1.name: Bulbasaur
1.flavorText: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.

2.name: Ivysaur
2.flavorText: When the bulb on its back grows large, it appears to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

3.name: Venusaur
3.flavorText: Its plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

4.name: Charmander
4.flavorText: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

5.name: Charmeleon
5.flavorText: It has a barbaric nature. In battle, it whips its fiery tail around and slashes away with sharp claws.

6.name: Charizard
6.flavorText: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

7.name: Squirtle
7.flavorText: When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.

8.name: Wartortle
8.flavorText: It is recognized as a symbol of longevity. If its shell has algae on it, that Wartortle is very old.

9.name: Blastoise
9.flavorText: It crushes its foe under its heavy body to cause fainting. In a pinch, it will withdraw inside its shell.

10.name: Caterpie
10.flavorText: For protection, it releases a horrible stench from the antenna on its head to drive away enemies.

11.name: Metapod
11.flavorText: It is waiting for the moment to evolve. At this stage, it can only harden, so it remains motionless to avoid attack.

12.name: Butterfree
12.flavorText: In battle, it flaps its wings at great speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

13.name: Weedle
13.flavorText: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

14.name: Kakuna
14.flavorText: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself when it is in danger.

15.name: Beedrill
15.flavorText: It has three poisonous stingers on its forelegs and its tail. They are used to jab its enemy repeatedly.

16.name: Pidgey
16.flavorText: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

17.name: Pidgeotto
17.flavorText: The claws on its feet are well developed. It can carry prey such as an Exeggcute to its nest over 60 miles away.

18.name: Pidgeot
18.flavorText: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

19.name: Rattata
19.flavorText: Its incisors grow continuously throughout its life. If its incisors get too long, this Pokémon becomes unable to eat, and it starves to death.

20.name: Raticate
20.flavorText: People say that it fled from its enemies by using its small webbed hind feet to swim from island to island in Alola.

21.name: Spearow
21.flavorText: Its reckless nature leads it to stand up to others—even large Pokémon—if it has to protect its territory.

22.name: Fearow
22.flavorText: Carrying food through Fearow’s territory is dangerous. It will snatch the food away from you in a flash!

23.name: Ekans
23.flavorText: By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

24.name: Arbok
24.flavorText: The latest research has determined that there are over 20 possible arrangements of the patterns on its stomach.

25.name: Pikachu
25.flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

26.name: Raichu
26.flavorText: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

27.name: Sandshrew
27.flavorText: It loves to bathe in the grit of dry, sandy areas. By sand bathing, the Pokémon rids itself of dirt and moisture clinging to its body.

28.name: Sandslash
28.flavorText: The drier the area Sandslash lives in, the harder and smoother the Pokémon’s spikes will feel when touched.

29.name: Nidoran-f
29.flavorText: Females are more sensitive to smells than males. While foraging, they’ll use their whiskers to check wind direction and stay downwind of predators.

30.name: Nidorina
30.flavorText: The horn on its head has atrophied. It’s thought that this happens so Nidorina’s children won’t get poked while their mother is feeding them.

31.name: Nidoqueen
31.flavorText: Nidoqueen is better at defense than offense. With scales like armor, this Pokémon will shield its children from any kind of attack.

32.name: Nidoran-m
32.flavorText: The horn on a male Nidoran’s forehead contains a powerful poison. This is a very cautious Pokémon, always straining its large ears.

33.name: Nidorino
33.flavorText: With a horn that’s harder than diamond, this Pokémon goes around shattering boulders as it searches for a moon stone.

34.name: Nidoking
34.flavorText: When it goes on a rampage, it’s impossible to control. But in the presence of a Nidoqueen it’s lived with for a long time, Nidoking calms down.

35.name: Clefairy
35.flavorText: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

36.name: Clefable
36.flavorText: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

37.name: Vulpix
37.flavorText: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

38.name: Ninetales
38.flavorText: It is said to live 1,000 years, and each of its tails is loaded with supernatural powers.

39.name: Jigglypuff
39.flavorText: Jigglypuff has top-notch lung capacity, even by comparison to other Pokémon. It won’t stop singing its lullabies until its foes fall asleep.

40.name: Wigglytuff
40.flavorText: The more air it takes in, the more it inflates. If opponents catch it in a bad mood, it will inflate itself to an enormous size to intimidate them.

41.name: Zubat
41.flavorText: It emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings. Even in tight caves, Zubat flies around with skill.

42.name: Golbat
42.flavorText: It loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.

43.name: Oddish
43.flavorText: If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.

44.name: Gloom
44.flavorText: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

45.name: Vileplume
45.flavorText: It has the world’s largest petals. With every step, the petals shake out heavy clouds of toxic pollen.

46.name: Paras
46.flavorText: Whether it’s due to a lack of moisture or a lack of nutrients, in Alola the mushrooms on Paras don’t grow up quite right.

47.name: Parasect
47.flavorText: The bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.

48.name: Venonat
48.flavorText: Its big eyes are actually clusters of tiny eyes. At night, its kind is drawn by light.

49.name: Venomoth
49.flavorText: The scales it scatters will paralyze anyone who touches them, making that person unable to stand.

50.name: Diglett
50.flavorText: If a Diglett digs through a field, it leaves the soil perfectly tilled and ideal for planting crops.

51.name: Dugtrio
51.flavorText: A team of Diglett triplets. It triggers huge earthquakes by burrowing 60 miles underground.

52.name: Meowth
52.flavorText: It loves to collect shiny things. If it’s in a good mood, it might even let its Trainer have a look at its hoard of treasures.

53.name: Persian
53.flavorText: Getting this prideful Pokémon to warm up to you takes a lot of effort, and it will claw at you the moment it gets annoyed.

54.name: Psyduck
54.flavorText: Psyduck is constantly beset by headaches. If the Pokémon lets its strange power erupt, apparently the pain subsides for a while.

55.name: Golduck
55.flavorText: This Pokémon lives in gently flowing rivers. It paddles through the water with its long limbs, putting its graceful swimming skills on display.

56.name: Mankey
56.flavorText: The smallest of things could cause it to lose its temper. Because it doesn’t hold in its stress, this Pokémon can live a long time.

57.name: Primeape
57.flavorText: It will never forgive opponents who have angered it. Even after it has beaten them down until they can’t move, it never ever forgives.

58.name: Growlithe
58.flavorText: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

59.name: Arcanine
59.flavorText: The sight of it running over 6,200 miles in a single day and night has captivated many people.

60.name: Poliwag
60.flavorText: For Poliwag, swimming is easier than walking. The swirl pattern on its belly is actually part of the Pokémon’s innards showing through the skin.

61.name: Poliwhirl
61.flavorText: Staring at the swirl on its belly causes drowsiness. This trait of Poliwhirl’s has been used in place of lullabies to get children to go to sleep.

62.name: Poliwrath
62.flavorText: Its body is solid muscle. When swimming through cold seas, Poliwrath uses its impressive arms to smash through drift ice and plow forward.

63.name: Abra
63.flavorText: This Pokémon uses its psychic powers while it sleeps. The contents of Abra’s dreams affect the powers that the Pokémon wields.

64.name: Kadabra
64.flavorText: Using its psychic power, Kadabra levitates as it sleeps. It uses its springy tail as a pillow.

65.name: Alakazam
65.flavorText: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.

66.name: Machop
66.flavorText: Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it’s the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

67.name: Machoke
67.flavorText: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

68.name: Machamp
68.flavorText: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

69.name: Bellsprout
69.flavorText: Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.

70.name: Weepinbell
70.flavorText: The leafy parts act as cutters for slashing foes. It spits a fluid that dissolves everything.

71.name: Victreebel
71.flavorText: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

72.name: Tentacool
72.flavorText: Tentacool is not a particularly strong swimmer. It drifts across the surface of shallow seas as it searches for prey.

73.name: Tentacruel
73.flavorText: When the red orbs on Tentacruel’s head glow brightly, watch out. The Pokémon is about to fire off a burst of ultrasonic waves.

74.name: Geodude
74.flavorText: Geodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they’re totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.

75.name: Graveler
75.flavorText: It climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.

76.name: Golem
76.flavorText: When Golem grow old, they stop shedding their shells. Those that have lived a long, long time have shells green with moss.

77.name: Ponyta
77.flavorText: It can’t run properly when it’s newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.

78.name: Rapidash
78.flavorText: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

79.name: Slowpoke
79.flavorText: Slow-witted and oblivious, this Pokémon won’t feel any pain if its tail gets eaten. It won’t notice when its tail grows back, either.

80.name: Slowbro
80.flavorText: Slowpoke became Slowbro when a Shellder bit on to its tail. Sweet flavors seeping from the tail make the Shellder feel as if its life is a dream.

81.name: Magnemite
81.flavorText: At times, Magnemite runs out of electricity and ends up on the ground. If you give batteries to a grounded Magnemite, it’ll start moving again.

82.name: Magneton
82.flavorText: This Pokémon is three Magnemite that have linked together. Magneton sends out powerful radio waves to study its surroundings.

83.name: Farfetchd
83.flavorText: The stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents. In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.

84.name: Doduo
84.flavorText: A two-headed Pokémon that was discovered as a sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.

85.name: Dodrio
85.flavorText: An enemy that takes its eyes off any of the three heads--even for a second--will get pecked severely.

86.name: Seel
86.flavorText: It has always been supposed that Seel live only in cold seas. Their having shown up in Alola is a mystery.

87.name: Dewgong
87.flavorText: It swims through the ocean at a speed of eight knots, searching for Pokémon that will become its prey. It’s especially fond of Wishiwashi.

88.name: Grimer
88.flavorText: It was born from sludge on the ocean floor. In a sterile environment, the germs within its body can’t multiply, and it dies.

89.name: Muk
89.flavorText: Their food sources have decreased, and their numbers have declined sharply. Sludge ponds are being built to prevent their extinction.

90.name: Shellder
90.flavorText: It swims facing backward by opening and closing its two-piece shell. It is surprisingly fast.

91.name: Cloyster
91.flavorText: Its shell is extremely hard. It cannot be shattered, even with a bomb. The shell opens only when it is attacking.

92.name: Gastly
92.flavorText: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

93.name: Haunter
93.flavorText: Its tongue is made of gas. If licked, its victim starts shaking constantly until death eventually comes.

94.name: Gengar
94.flavorText: On the night of a full moon, if shadows move on their own and laugh, it must be Gengar’s doing.

95.name: Onix
95.flavorText: As it digs through the ground, it absorbs many hard objects. This is what makes its body so solid.

96.name: Drowzee
96.flavorText: It can be spotted near recreational facilities, intending to eat the pleasant dreams of children who enjoyed themselves there that day.

97.name: Hypno
97.flavorText: In Alola, Komala is Hypno’s main target. It rarely harms people.

98.name: Krabby
98.flavorText: It can be found near the sea. The large pincers grow back if they are torn out of their sockets.

99.name: Kingler
99.flavorText: Its large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.

100.name: Voltorb
100.flavorText: It was discovered when Poké Balls were introduced. It is said that there is some connection.

101.name: Electrode
101.flavorText: It explodes in response to even minor stimuli. It is feared, with the nickname of “The Bomb Ball.”

102.name: Exeggcute
102.flavorText: Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

103.name: Exeggutor
103.flavorText: Each of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.

104.name: Cubone
104.flavorText: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

105.name: Marowak
105.flavorText: This Pokémon overcame its sorrow to evolve a sturdy new body. Marowak faces its opponents bravely, using a bone as a weapon.

106.name: Hitmonlee
106.flavorText: This amazing Pokémon has an awesome sense of balance. It can kick in succession from any position.

107.name: Hitmonchan
107.flavorText: Its punches slice the air. They are launched at such high speed, even a slight graze could cause a burn.

108.name: Lickitung
108.flavorText: If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.

109.name: Koffing
109.flavorText: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

110.name: Weezing
110.flavorText: It mixes gases between its two bodies. It’s said that these Pokémon were seen all over the Galar region back in the day.

111.name: Rhyhorn
111.flavorText: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging tackles.

112.name: Rhydon
112.flavorText: It begins walking on its hind legs after evolution. It can punch holes through boulders with its horn.

113.name: Chansey
113.flavorText: The egg Chansey carries is not only delicious but also packed with nutrition. It’s used as a high-class cooking ingredient.

114.name: Tangela
114.flavorText: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

115.name: Kangaskhan
115.flavorText: Although it’s carrying its baby in a pouch on its belly, Kangaskhan is swift on its feet. It intimidates its opponents with quick jabs.

116.name: Horsea
116.flavorText: Horsea makes its home in oceans with gentle currents. If this Pokémon is under attack, it spits out pitch-black ink and escapes.

117.name: Seadra
117.flavorText: It’s the males that raise the offspring. While Seadra are raising young, the spines on their backs secrete thicker and stronger poison.

118.name: Goldeen
118.flavorText: Its dorsal, pectoral, and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the Water Dancer.

119.name: Seaking
119.flavorText: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

120.name: Staryu
120.flavorText: If you visit a beach at the end of summer, you’ll be able to see groups of Staryu lighting up in a steady rhythm.

121.name: Starmie
121.flavorText: This Pokémon has an organ known as its core. The organ glows in seven colors when Starmie is unleashing its potent psychic powers.

122.name: Mr-mime
122.flavorText: The broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.

123.name: Scyther
123.flavorText: As Scyther fights more and more battles, its scythes become sharper and sharper. With a single slice, Scyther can fell a massive tree.

124.name: Jynx
124.flavorText: In certain parts of Galar, Jynx was once feared and worshiped as the Queen of Ice.

125.name: Electabuzz
125.flavorText: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

126.name: Magmar
126.flavorText: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

127.name: Pinsir
127.flavorText: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

128.name: Tauros
128.flavorText: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

129.name: Magikarp
129.flavorText: It is virtually worthless in terms of both power and speed. It is the most weak and pathetic Pokémon in the world.

130.name: Gyarados
130.flavorText: It has an extremely aggressive nature. The Hyper Beam it shoots from its mouth totally incinerates all targets.

131.name: Lapras
131.flavorText: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

132.name: Ditto
132.flavorText: It can reconstitute its entire cellular structure to change into what it sees, but it returns to normal when it relaxes.

133.name: Eevee
133.flavorText: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

134.name: Vaporeon
134.flavorText: When Vaporeon’s fins begin to vibrate, it is a sign that rain will come within a few hours.

135.name: Jolteon
135.flavorText: If it is angered or startled, the fur all over its body bristles like sharp needles that pierce foes.

136.name: Flareon
136.flavorText: Once it has stored up enough heat, this Pokémon’s body temperature can reach up to 1,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

137.name: Porygon
137.flavorText: State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.

138.name: Omanyte
138.flavorText: Because some Omanyte manage to escape after being restored or are released into the wild by people, this species is becoming a problem.

139.name: Omastar
139.flavorText: Weighed down by a large and heavy shell, Omastar couldn’t move very fast. Some say it went extinct because it was unable to catch food.

140.name: Kabuto
140.flavorText: This species is almost entirely extinct. Kabuto molt every three days, making their shells harder and harder.

141.name: Kabutops
141.flavorText: Kabutops slices its prey apart and sucks out the fluids. The discarded body parts become food for other Pokémon.

142.name: Aerodactyl
142.flavorText: This is a ferocious Pokémon from ancient times. Apparently even modern technology is incapable of producing a perfectly restored specimen.

143.name: Snorlax
143.flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

144.name: Articuno
144.flavorText: It’s said that this Pokémon’s beautiful blue wings are made of ice. Articuno flies over snowy mountains, its long tail fluttering along behind it.

145.name: Zapdos
145.flavorText: This Pokémon has complete control over electricity. There are tales of Zapdos nesting in the dark depths of pitch-black thunderclouds.

146.name: Moltres
146.flavorText: It’s one of the legendary bird Pokémon. When Moltres flaps its flaming wings, they glimmer with a dazzling red glow.

147.name: Dratini
147.flavorText: Dratini dwells near bodies of rapidly flowing water, such as the plunge pools of waterfalls. As it grows, Dratini will shed its skin many times.

148.name: Dragonair
148.flavorText: This Pokémon lives in pristine oceans and lakes. It can control the weather, and it uses this power to fly into the sky, riding on the wind.

149.name: Dragonite
149.flavorText: It’s a kindhearted Pokémon. If it spots a drowning person or Pokémon, Dragonite simply must help them.

150.name: Mewtwo
150.flavorText: It was created by a scientist after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA-engineering experiments.

151.name: Mew
151.flavorText: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe Mew to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

152.name: Chikorita
152.flavorText: A sweet aroma gently wafts from the leaf on its head. It is docile and loves to soak up sun rays.

153.name: Bayleef
153.flavorText: A spicy aroma emanates from around its neck. The aroma acts as a stimulant to restore health.

154.name: Meganium
154.flavorText: The aroma that rises from its petals contains a substance that calms aggressive feelings.

155.name: Cyndaquil
155.flavorText: It is timid and always curls itself up in a ball. If attacked, it flares up its back for protection.

156.name: Quilava
156.flavorText: Before battle, it turns its back on its opponent to demonstrate how ferociously its fire blazes.

157.name: Typhlosion
157.flavorText: If its rage peaks, it becomes so hot that anything that touches it will instantly go up in flames.

158.name: Totodile
158.flavorText: It is small but rough and tough. It won’t hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

159.name: Croconaw
159.flavorText: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

160.name: Feraligatr
160.flavorText: It usually moves slowly, but it goes at blinding speed when it attacks and bites prey.

161.name: Sentret
161.flavorText: When acting as a lookout, it warns others of danger by screeching and hitting the ground with its tail.

162.name: Furret
162.flavorText: The mother puts its offspring to sleep by curling up around them. It corners foes with speed.

163.name: Hoothoot
163.flavorText: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

164.name: Noctowl
164.flavorText: Its eyes are specially developed to enable it to see clearly even in murky darkness and minimal light.

165.name: Ledyba
165.flavorText: This Pokémon is very sensitive to cold. In the warmth of Alola, it appears quite lively.

166.name: Ledian
166.flavorText: It’s said that the patterns on its back are related to the stars in the night sky, but the details of that relationship remain unclear.

167.name: Spinarak
167.flavorText: With threads from its mouth, it fashions sturdy webs that won’t break even if you set a rock on them.

168.name: Ariados
168.flavorText: Every night, it wanders around in search of prey, whose movements it restrains by spewing threads before it bites into them with its fangs.

169.name: Crobat
169.flavorText: Both of its legs have turned into wings. Without a sound, Crobat flies swiftly toward its prey and sinks its fangs into the nape of its target’s neck.

170.name: Chinchou
170.flavorText: Its antennae, which evolved from a fin, have both positive and negative charges flowing through them.

171.name: Lanturn
171.flavorText: The light it emits is so bright that it can illuminate the sea’s surface from a depth of over three miles.

172.name: Pichu
172.flavorText: Despite its small size, it can zap even adult humans. However, if it does so, it also surprises itself.

173.name: Cleffa
173.flavorText: According to local rumors, Cleffa are often seen in places where shooting stars have fallen.

174.name: Igglybuff
174.flavorText: Igglybuff loves to sing. Its marshmallow-like body gives off a faint sweet smell.

175.name: Togepi
175.flavorText: The shell seems to be filled with joy. It is said that it will share good luck when treated kindly.

176.name: Togetic
176.flavorText: They say that it will appear before kindhearted, caring people and shower them with happiness.

177.name: Natu
177.flavorText: It is extremely good at climbing tree trunks and likes to eat the new sprouts on the trees.

178.name: Xatu
178.flavorText: They say that it stays still and quiet because it is seeing both the past and future at the same time.

179.name: Mareep
179.flavorText: Clothing made from Mareep’s fleece is easily charged with static electricity, so a special process is used on it.

180.name: Flaaffy
180.flavorText: In the places on its body where fleece doesn’t grow, its skin is rubbery and doesn’t conduct electricity. Those spots are safe to touch.

181.name: Ampharos
181.flavorText: The light from its tail can be seen from space. This is why you can always tell exactly where it is, which is why it usually keeps the light off.

182.name: Bellossom
182.flavorText: Plentiful in the tropics. When it dances, its petals rub together and make a pleasant ringing sound.

183.name: Marill
183.flavorText: This Pokémon uses its round tail as a float. The ball of Marill’s tail is filled with nutrients that have been turned into an oil.

184.name: Azumarill
184.flavorText: It spends most of its time in the water. On sunny days, Azumarill floats on the surface of the water and sunbathes.

185.name: Sudowoodo
185.flavorText: If a tree branch shakes when there is no wind, it’s a Sudowoodo, not a tree. It hides from the rain.

186.name: Politoed
186.flavorText: At nightfall, these Pokémon appear on the shores of lakes. They announce their territorial claims by letting out cries that sound like shouting.

187.name: Hoppip
187.flavorText: Its body is so light, it must grip the ground firmly with its feet to keep from being blown away.

188.name: Skiploom
188.flavorText: The bloom on top of its head opens and closes as the temperature fluctuates up and down.

189.name: Jumpluff
189.flavorText: Blown by seasonal winds, it circles the globe, scattering cotton spores as it goes.

190.name: Aipom
190.flavorText: As it did more and more with its tail, its hands became clumsy. It makes its nest high in the treetops.

191.name: Sunkern
191.flavorText: It may plummet from the sky. If attacked by a Spearow, it will violently shake its leaves.

192.name: Sunflora
192.flavorText: As the hot season approaches, the petals on this Pokémon’s face become more vivid and lively.

193.name: Yanma
193.flavorText: Its eyes can see 360 degrees without moving its head. It won’t miss prey--even those behind it.

194.name: Wooper
194.flavorText: This Pokémon lives in cold water. It will leave the water to search for food when it gets cold outside.

195.name: Quagsire
195.flavorText: It has an easygoing nature. It doesn’t care if it bumps its head on boats and boulders while swimming.

196.name: Espeon
196.flavorText: By reading air currents, it can predict things such as the weather or its foe’s next move.

197.name: Umbreon
197.flavorText: When this Pokémon becomes angry, its pores secrete a poisonous sweat, which it sprays at its opponent’s eyes.

198.name: Murkrow
198.flavorText: It has a weakness for shiny things. It’s been known to sneak into the nests of Gabite—noted collectors of jewels—in search of treasure.

199.name: Slowking
199.flavorText: Miraculously, this former Slowpoke’s latent intelligence was drawn out when Shellder poison raced through its brain.

200.name: Misdreavus
200.flavorText: What gives meaning to its life is surprising others. If you set your ear against the red orbs around its neck, you can hear shrieking.

201.name: Unown
201.flavorText: Its flat, thin body is always stuck on walls. Its shape appears to have some meaning.

202.name: Wobbuffet
202.flavorText: It hates light and shock. If attacked, it inflates its body to pump up its counterstrike.

203.name: Girafarig
203.flavorText: While it sleeps, the head on its tail keeps watch. The tail doesn’t need to sleep.

204.name: Pineco
204.flavorText: Motionless, it hangs from trees, waiting for its bug Pokémon prey to come to it. Its favorite in Alola is Cutiefly.

205.name: Forretress
205.flavorText: When something approaches it, it fires off fragments of its steel shell in attack. This is not a conscious action but a conditioned reflex.

206.name: Dunsparce
206.flavorText: This Pokémon’s tiny wings have some scientists saying that Dunsparce used to fly through the sky in ancient times.

207.name: Gligar
207.flavorText: It usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.

208.name: Steelix
208.flavorText: It is said that if an Onix lives for over 100 years, its composition changes to become diamond-like.

209.name: Snubbull
209.flavorText: It grows close to others easily and is also easily spoiled. The disparity between its face and its actions makes many young people wild about it.

210.name: Granbull
210.flavorText: While it has powerful jaws, it doesn’t care for disputes, so it rarely has a chance to display their might.

211.name: Qwilfish
211.flavorText: When faced with a larger opponent, it swallows as much water as it can to match the opponent’s size.

212.name: Scizor
212.flavorText: Bulky pincers account for one third of Scizor’s body weight. A single swing of one of these pincers will crush a boulder completely.

213.name: Shuckle
213.flavorText: It stores berries inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.

214.name: Heracross
214.flavorText: Heracross loves sweet sap and will go looking through forests for it. The Pokémon uses its two antennae to pick up scents as it searches.

215.name: Sneasel
215.flavorText: Its paws conceal sharp claws. If attacked, it suddenly extends the claws and startles its enemy.

216.name: Teddiursa
216.flavorText: If it finds honey, its crescent mark glows. It always licks its paws because they’re soaked with honey.

217.name: Ursaring
217.flavorText: With its ability to distinguish any aroma, it unfailingly finds all food buried deep underground.

218.name: Slugma
218.flavorText: A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

219.name: Magcargo
219.flavorText: Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.

220.name: Swinub
220.flavorText: It rubs its snout on the ground to find and dig up food. It sometimes discovers hot springs.

221.name: Piloswine
221.flavorText: If it charges at an enemy, the hairs on its back stand up straight. It is very sensitive to sound.

222.name: Corsola
222.flavorText: It will regrow any branches that break off its head. People keep particularly beautiful Corsola branches as charms to promote safe childbirth.

223.name: Remoraid
223.flavorText: The water they shoot from their mouths can hit moving prey from more than 300 feet away.

224.name: Octillery
224.flavorText: It has a tendency to want to be in holes. It prefers rock crags or pots and sprays ink from them before attacking.

225.name: Delibird
225.flavorText: It carries food all day long. There are tales about lost people who were saved by the food it had.

226.name: Mantine
226.flavorText: If it builds up enough speed swimming, it can jump out above the waves and glide for over 300 feet.

227.name: Skarmory
227.flavorText: The pointed feathers of these Pokémon are sharper than swords. Skarmory and Corviknight fight viciously over territory.

228.name: Houndour
228.flavorText: It cooperates with others skillfully. When it becomes your partner, it’s very loyal to you as its Trainer and will obey your orders.

229.name: Houndoom
229.flavorText: They spew flames mixed with poison to finish off their opponents. They divvy up their prey evenly among the members of their pack.

230.name: Kingdra
230.flavorText: With the arrival of a storm at sea, this Pokémon will show itself on the surface. When a Kingdra and a Dragonite meet, a fierce battle ensues.

231.name: Phanpy
231.flavorText: As a sign of affection, it bumps with its snout. However, it is so strong, it may send you flying.

232.name: Donphan
232.flavorText: It has sharp, hard tusks and a rugged hide. Its Tackle is strong enough to knock down a house.

233.name: Porygon2
233.flavorText: This is a Porygon that was updated with special data. Porygon2 develops itself by learning about many different subjects all on its own.

234.name: Stantler
234.flavorText: Staring at its antlers creates an odd sensation as if one were being drawn into their centers.

235.name: Smeargle
235.flavorText: The fluid of Smeargle’s tail secretions changes in the intensity of its hue as the Pokémon’s emotions change.

236.name: Tyrogue
236.flavorText: It is always bursting with energy. To make itself stronger, it keeps on fighting even if it loses.

237.name: Hitmontop
237.flavorText: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

238.name: Smoochum
238.flavorText: If its face gets even slightly dirty, Smoochum will bathe immediately. But if its body gets dirty, Smoochum doesn’t really seem to care.

239.name: Elekid
239.flavorText: When a storm approaches, this Pokémon gets restless. Once Elekid hears the sound of thunder, it gets full-on rowdy.

240.name: Magby
240.flavorText: This Pokémon is still small and timid. Whenever Magby gets excited or surprised, flames leak from its mouth and its nose.

241.name: Miltank
241.flavorText: Miltank produces highly nutritious milk, so it’s been supporting the lives of people and other Pokémon since ancient times.

242.name: Blissey
242.flavorText: Whenever a Blissey finds a weakened Pokémon, it will share its egg and offer its care until the other Pokémon is all better.

243.name: Raikou
243.flavorText: The rain clouds it carries let it fire thunderbolts at will. They say that it descended with lightning.

244.name: Entei
244.flavorText: A Pokémon that races across the land. It is said that one is born every time a new volcano appears.

245.name: Suicune
245.flavorText: It races around the world to purify fouled water. It dashes away with the north wind.

246.name: Larvitar
246.flavorText: Born deep underground, it comes aboveground and becomes a pupa once it has finished eating the surrounding soil.

247.name: Pupitar
247.flavorText: Even sealed in its shell, it can move freely. Hard and fast, it has outstanding destructive power.

248.name: Tyranitar
248.flavorText: Its body can’t be harmed by any sort of attack, so it is very eager to make challenges against enemies.

249.name: Lugia
249.flavorText: It sleeps in a deep-sea trench. If it flaps its wings, it is said to cause a 40-day storm.

250.name: Ho-oh
250.flavorText: A legend says that its body glows in seven colors. A rainbow is said to form behind it when it flies.

251.name: Celebi
251.flavorText: This Pokémon has the ability to move through time. Records describing it as a forest deity can be found from many different eras.

252.name: Treecko
252.flavorText: It quickly scales even vertical walls. It senses humidity with its tail to predict the next day’s weather.

253.name: Grovyle
253.flavorText: Its strongly developed thigh muscles give it astounding agility and jumping performance.

254.name: Sceptile
254.flavorText: The leaves that grow on its arms can slice down thick trees. It is without peer in jungle combat.

255.name: Torchic
255.flavorText: It has a flame sac inside its belly that perpetually burns. It feels warm if it is hugged.

256.name: Combusken
256.flavorText: Its kicking mastery lets it loose 10 kicks per second. It emits sharp cries to intimidate foes.

257.name: Blaziken
257.flavorText: It can clear a 30-story building in a leap. Its fiery punches scorch its foes.

258.name: Mudkip
258.flavorText: Its large tail fin propels it through water with powerful acceleration. It is strong in spite of its size.

259.name: Marshtomp
259.flavorText: Its sturdy legs give it sure footing, even in mud. It burrows into dirt to sleep.

260.name: Swampert
260.flavorText: Its arms are hard as rock. With one swing, it can break a boulder into pieces.

261.name: Poochyena
261.flavorText: A Pokémon with a persistent nature, it chases its chosen prey until the prey becomes exhausted.

262.name: Mightyena
262.flavorText: It will always obey the commands of a skilled Trainer. Its behavior arises from its living in packs in ancient times.

263.name: Zigzagoon
263.flavorText: It marks its territory by rubbing its bristly fur on trees. This variety of Zigzagoon is friendlier and calmer than the kind native to Galar.

264.name: Linoone
264.flavorText: Its fur is strong and supple. Shaving brushes made with shed Linoone hairs are highly prized.

265.name: Wurmple
265.flavorText: It loves to eat leaves. If it is attacked by a Starly, it will defend itself with its spiked rear.

266.name: Silcoon
266.flavorText: It wraps silk around the branches of a tree. It drinks rainwater on its silk while awaiting evolution.

267.name: Beautifly
267.flavorText: Vibrantly patterned wings are its prominent feature. It sucks sweet flower nectar with its long mouth.

268.name: Cascoon
268.flavorText: Its body, which is made of soft silk, hardens over time. When cracks appear, evolution is near.

269.name: Dustox
269.flavorText: A nocturnal Pokémon. Drawn by streetlights, they messily eat the leaves of trees lining boulevards.

270.name: Lotad
270.flavorText: It searches about for clean water. If it does not drink water for too long, the leaf on its head wilts.

271.name: Lombre
271.flavorText: It is nocturnal and becomes active at nightfall. It feeds on aquatic mosses that grow in the riverbed.

272.name: Ludicolo
272.flavorText: The rhythm of bright, festive music activates Ludicolo’s cells, making it more powerful.

273.name: Seedot
273.flavorText: If it remains still, it looks just like a real nut. It delights in surprising foraging Pokémon.

274.name: Nuzleaf
274.flavorText: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

275.name: Shiftry
275.flavorText: A Pokémon that was feared as a forest guardian. It can read the foe’s mind and take preemptive action.

276.name: Taillow
276.flavorText: It has a gutsy spirit that makes it bravely take on tough foes. It flies in search of warm climates.

277.name: Swellow
277.flavorText: If its two tail feathers are standing at attention, it is proof of good health. It soars elegantly in the sky.

278.name: Wingull
278.flavorText: It makes its nest on sheer cliffs. Riding the sea breeze, it glides up into the expansive skies.

279.name: Pelipper
279.flavorText: It is a messenger of the skies, carrying small Pokémon and eggs to safety in its bill.

280.name: Ralts
280.flavorText: It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

281.name: Kirlia
281.flavorText: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

282.name: Gardevoir
282.flavorText: It has the power to predict the future. Its power peaks when it is protecting its Trainer.

283.name: Surskit
283.flavorText: If it’s in a pinch, it will secrete a sweet liquid from the tip of its head. Syrup made from gathering that liquid is tasty on bread.

284.name: Masquerain
284.flavorText: Masquerain intimidates enemies with the eyelike patterns of its eyespots. If that doesn’t work, it deftly makes its escape on its set of four wings.

285.name: Shroomish
285.flavorText: It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.

286.name: Breloom
286.flavorText: Its short arms stretch when it throws punches. Its technique is equal to that of pro boxers.

287.name: Slakoth
287.flavorText: The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who watches it feel like doing the same.

288.name: Vigoroth
288.flavorText: Its heartbeat is fast and its blood so agitated that it can’t sit still for one second.

289.name: Slaking
289.flavorText: The world’s laziest Pokémon. It moves to another spot when there’s no food left within its reach.

290.name: Nincada
290.flavorText: Because it lived almost entirely underground, it is nearly blind. It uses its antennae instead.

291.name: Ninjask
291.flavorText: Its cry leaves a lasting headache if heard for too long. It moves so quickly that it is almost invisible.

292.name: Shedinja
292.flavorText: A most peculiar Pokémon that somehow appears in a Poké Ball when a Nincada evolves.

293.name: Whismur
293.flavorText: The cry of a Whismur is over 100 decibels. If you’re close to a Whismur when it lets out a cry, you’ll be stuck with an all-day headache.

294.name: Loudred
294.flavorText: Loudred’s ears serve as speakers, and they can put out sound waves powerful enough to blow away a house.

295.name: Exploud
295.flavorText: In the past, people would use the loud voices of these Pokémon as a means of communication between distant cities.

296.name: Makuhita
296.flavorText: It practices its slaps by repeatedly slapping tree trunks. It has been known to slap an Exeggutor and get flung away.

297.name: Hariyama
297.flavorText: Although they enjoy comparing their strength, they’re also kind. They value etiquette, praising opponents they battle.

298.name: Azurill
298.flavorText: The ball on Azurill’s tail bounces like a rubber ball, and it’s full of the nutrients the Pokémon needs to grow.

299.name: Nosepass
299.flavorText: It moves less than an inch a year, but when it’s in a jam, it will spin and drill down into the ground in a split second.

300.name: Skitty
300.flavorText: It can’t stop itself from chasing moving things, and it runs in a circle, chasing its own tail.

301.name: Delcatty
301.flavorText: It dislikes dirty places. It often searches for a comfortable place in which to groom itself.

302.name: Sableye
302.flavorText: This Pokémon is feared. When its gemstone eyes begin to glow with a sinister shine, it’s believed that Sableye will steal people’s spirits away.

303.name: Mawile
303.flavorText: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

304.name: Aron
304.flavorText: It eats iron ore—and sometimes railroad tracks— to build up the steel armor that protects its body.

305.name: Lairon
305.flavorText: Lairon live in mountains brimming with spring water and iron ore, so these Pokémon often came into conflict with humans in the past.

306.name: Aggron
306.flavorText: Aggron has a horn sharp enough to perforate thick iron sheets. It brings down its opponents by ramming into them horn first.

307.name: Meditite
307.flavorText: It always trains deep in mountains. It levitates when it heightens its spiritual power through meditation.

308.name: Medicham
308.flavorText: It elegantly avoids attacks with dance-like steps, then launches a devastating blow in the same motion.

309.name: Electrike
309.flavorText: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

310.name: Manectric
310.flavorText: It stimulates its own muscles with electricity, so it can move quickly. It eases its soreness with electricity, too, so it can recover quickly as well.

311.name: Plusle
311.flavorText: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

312.name: Minun
312.flavorText: Exposure to electricity from Minun and Plusle promotes blood circulation and relaxes muscles.

313.name: Volbeat
313.flavorText: It communicates with others by lighting up its rear at night. It loves Illumise’s sweet aroma.

314.name: Illumise
314.flavorText: Its fragrance attracts a swarm of Volbeat, so they draw over 200 patterns in the night sky.

315.name: Roselia
315.flavorText: Its flowers give off a relaxing fragrance. The stronger its aroma, the healthier the Roselia is.

316.name: Gulpin
316.flavorText: It has a small heart and brain. Its stomach comprises most of its body, with enzymes to dissolve anything.

317.name: Swalot
317.flavorText: It swallows anything whole. It sweats toxic fluids from its follicles to douse foes.

318.name: Carvanha
318.flavorText: It won’t attack while it’s alone—not even if it spots prey. Instead, it waits for other Carvanha to join it, and then the Pokémon attack as a group.

319.name: Sharpedo
319.flavorText: As soon as it catches the scent of prey, Sharpedo will jet seawater from its backside, hurtling toward the target to attack at 75 mph.

320.name: Wailmer
320.flavorText: It shows off by spraying jets of seawater from the nostrils above its eyes. It eats a solid ton of Wishiwashi every day.

321.name: Wailord
321.flavorText: It can sometimes knock out opponents with the shock created by breaching and crashing its big body onto the water.

322.name: Numel
322.flavorText: Its humped back stores intensely hot magma. In rain, the magma cools, slowing its movement.

323.name: Camerupt
323.flavorText: The volcanoes on its back have a major eruption every 10 years--or whenever it becomes really angry.

324.name: Torkoal
324.flavorText: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

325.name: Spoink
325.flavorText: It bounces constantly, using its tail like a spring. The shock of bouncing keeps its heart beating.

326.name: Grumpig
326.flavorText: It uses black pearls to amplify its psychic power. It does a strange dance to control foes’ minds.

327.name: Spinda
327.flavorText: Its steps are shaky and stumbling. Walking for a long time makes it feel sick.

328.name: Trapinch
328.flavorText: Its nest is a sloped, bowl-like pit in the desert. Once something has fallen in, there is no escape.

329.name: Vibrava
329.flavorText: The ultrasonic waves it generates by rubbing its two wings together cause severe headaches.

330.name: Flygon
330.flavorText: This Pokémon hides in the heart of sandstorms it creates and seldom appears where people can see it.

331.name: Cacnea
331.flavorText: It lives in arid locations. Its yellow flowers bloom once a year.

332.name: Cacturne
332.flavorText: It becomes active at night, seeking prey that is exhausted from the day’s desert heat.

333.name: Swablu
333.flavorText: Its cottony wings are full of air, making them light and fluffy to the touch. Swablu takes diligent care of its wings.

334.name: Altaria
334.flavorText: As it flies in a calm and relaxed manner, Altaria performs a humming song that would enrapture any audience.

335.name: Zangoose
335.flavorText: It has feuded with Seviper for many generations. Its sharp claws are its biggest weapons.

336.name: Seviper
336.flavorText: In battle, it uses its bladed tail to counter any Zangoose. It secretes a deadly venom in its tail.

337.name: Lunatone
337.flavorText: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It’s active on the night of a full moon.

338.name: Solrock
338.flavorText: When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.

339.name: Barboach
339.flavorText: Its slimy body is hard to grasp. In one region, it is said to have been born from hardened mud.

340.name: Whiscash
340.flavorText: It makes its nest at the bottom of swamps. It will eat anything—if it is alive, Whiscash will eat it.

341.name: Corphish
341.flavorText: No matter how dirty the water in the river, it will adapt and thrive. It has a strong will to survive.

342.name: Crawdaunt
342.flavorText: A rough customer that wildly flails its giant claws. It is said to be extremely hard to raise.

343.name: Baltoy
343.flavorText: It moves while spinning around on its single foot. Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on their heads.

344.name: Claydol
344.flavorText: This mysterious Pokémon started life as an ancient clay figurine made over 20,000 years ago.

345.name: Lileep
345.flavorText: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. Lileep once lived in warm seas that existed approximately 100,000,000 years ago.

346.name: Cradily
346.flavorText: It has short legs and can’t walk very fast, but its neck and tentacles can extend to over three times their usual length to nab distant prey.

347.name: Anorith
347.flavorText: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. Anorith lived in the ocean about 100,000,000 years ago, hunting with its pair of claws.

348.name: Armaldo
348.flavorText: After evolution, this Pokémon emerged onto land. Its lower body has become stronger, and blows from its tail are devastating.

349.name: Feebas
349.flavorText: Although unattractive and unpopular, this Pokémon’s marvelous vitality has made it a subject of research.

350.name: Milotic
350.flavorText: Milotic has provided inspiration to many artists. It has even been referred to as the most beautiful Pokémon of all.

351.name: Castform
351.flavorText: Although its form changes with the weather, that is apparently the result of a chemical reaction and not the result of its own free will.

352.name: Kecleon
352.flavorText: It changes its hue to blend into its surroundings. If no one takes notice of it for too long, it will pout and never reveal itself.

353.name: Shuppet
353.flavorText: It eats up emotions like malice, jealousy, and resentment, so some people are grateful for its presence.

354.name: Banette
354.flavorText: It’s a stuffed toy that was thrown away and became possessed, ever searching for the one who threw it away so it can exact its revenge.

355.name: Duskull
355.flavorText: If it finds bad children who won’t listen to their parents, it will spirit them away—or so it’s said.

356.name: Dusclops
356.flavorText: Its body is entirely hollow. When it opens its mouth, it sucks everything in as if it were a black hole.

357.name: Tropius
357.flavorText: The bunches of fruit growing around the necks of Tropius in Alola are especially sweet compared to those in other regions.

358.name: Chimecho
358.flavorText: It uses the sucker on its head to hang from a tree or from eaves. It can produce seven different tones.

359.name: Absol
359.flavorText: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

360.name: Wynaut
360.flavorText: It tends to move in a pack. Individuals squash against one another to toughen their spirits.

361.name: Snorunt
361.flavorText: It’s said that if they are seen at midnight, they’ll cause heavy snow. They eat snow and ice to survive.

362.name: Glalie
362.flavorText: It has a body of ice that won’t melt, even with fire. It can instantly freeze moisture in the atmosphere.

363.name: Spheal
363.flavorText: This Pokémon’s body is covered in blubber and impressively round. It’s faster for Spheal to roll around than walk.

364.name: Sealeo
364.flavorText: Sealeo live on top of drift ice. They go swimming when they’re on the hunt, seeking out their prey by scent.

365.name: Walrein
365.flavorText: Walrein form herds of 20 to 30 individuals. When a threat appears, the herd’s leader will protect the group with its life.

366.name: Clamperl
366.flavorText: Despite its appearance, it’s carnivorous. It clamps down on its prey with both sides of its shell and doesn’t let go until they stop moving.

367.name: Huntail
367.flavorText: It’s not the strongest swimmer. It wags its tail to lure in its prey and then gulps them down as soon as they get close.

368.name: Gorebyss
368.flavorText: The color of its body changes with the water temperature. The coloration of Gorebyss in Alola is almost blindingly vivid.

369.name: Relicanth
369.flavorText: Rock-hard scales and oil-filled swim bladders allow this Pokémon to survive the intense water pressure of the deep sea.

370.name: Luvdisc
370.flavorText: There was an era when it was overfished due to the rumor that having one of its heart-shaped scales would enable you to find a sweetheart.

371.name: Bagon
371.flavorText: Bagon jumps off cliffs every day, trying to grow stronger so that someday it will be able to fly.

372.name: Shelgon
372.flavorText: This Pokémon has covered its body in a hard shell that has the same composition as bone. Shelgon stores energy for evolution.

373.name: Salamence
373.flavorText: Salamence is an unusual Pokémon in that it was able to evolve a body with wings just by constantly wishing to be able to fly.

374.name: Beldum
374.flavorText: From its rear, Beldum emits a magnetic force that rapidly pulls opponents in. They get skewered on Beldum’s sharp claws.

375.name: Metang
375.flavorText: Two Beldum have become stuck together via their own magnetic forces. With two brains, the resulting Metang has doubled psychic powers.

376.name: Metagross
376.flavorText: Because the magnetic powers of these Pokémon get stronger in freezing temperatures, Metagross living on snowy mountains are full of energy.

377.name: Regirock
377.flavorText: Every bit of Regirock’s body is made of stone. As parts of its body erode, this Pokémon sticks rocks to itself to repair what’s been lost.

378.name: Regice
378.flavorText: With cold air that can reach temperatures as low as −328 degrees Fahrenheit, Regice instantly freezes any creature that approaches it.

379.name: Registeel
379.flavorText: Registeel’s body is made of a strange material that is flexible enough to stretch and shrink but also more durable than any metal.

380.name: Latias
380.flavorText: It communicates using telepathy. Its body is covered in down that refracts light to make it invisible.

381.name: Latios
381.flavorText: A highly intelligent Pokémon. By folding back its wings in flight, it can overtake jet planes.

382.name: Kyogre
382.flavorText: A mythical Pokémon said to have swelled the seas with rain and tidal waves. It battled with Groudon.

383.name: Groudon
383.flavorText: Said to have expanded the lands by evaporating water with raging heat. It battled titanically with Kyogre.

384.name: Rayquaza
384.flavorText: It lives in the ozone layer far above the clouds and cannot be seen from the ground.

385.name: Jirachi
385.flavorText: Once every 1,000 years, the singing of a pure voice will rouse this Pokémon from its near- perpetual slumber. It wakes for only seven days.

386.name: Deoxys-normal
386.flavorText: An alien virus that fell to earth on a meteor underwent a DNA mutation to become this Pokémon.

387.name: Turtwig
387.flavorText: Photosynthesis occurs across its body under the sun. The shell on its back is actually hardened soil.

388.name: Grotle
388.flavorText: It lives along water in forests. In the daytime, it leaves the forest to sunbathe its treed shell.

389.name: Torterra
389.flavorText: Ancient people imagined that beneath the ground, a gigantic Torterra dwelled.

390.name: Chimchar
390.flavorText: Its fiery rear end is fueled by gas made in its belly. Even rain can’t extinguish the fire.

391.name: Monferno
391.flavorText: It skillfully controls the intensity of the fire on its tail to keep its foes at an ideal distance.

392.name: Infernape
392.flavorText: Its crown of fire is indicative of its fiery nature. It is beaten by none in terms of quickness.

393.name: Piplup
393.flavorText: It doesn’t like to be taken care of. It’s difficult to bond with since it won’t listen to its Trainer.

394.name: Prinplup
394.flavorText: It lives alone, away from others. Apparently, every one of them believes it is the most important.

395.name: Empoleon
395.flavorText: It swims as fast as a jet boat. The edges of its wings are sharp and can slice apart drifting ice.

396.name: Starly
396.flavorText: They flock in great numbers. Though small, they flap their wings with great power.

397.name: Staravia
397.flavorText: They maintain huge flocks, although fierce scuffles break out between various flocks.

398.name: Staraptor
398.flavorText: The muscles in its wings and legs are strong. It can easily fly while gripping a small Pokémon.

399.name: Bidoof
399.flavorText: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

400.name: Bibarel
400.flavorText: It busily makes its nest with stacks of branches and roots it has cut up with its sharp incisors.

401.name: Kricketot
401.flavorText: It chats with others using the sounds of its colliding antennae. These sounds are fall hallmarks.

402.name: Kricketune
402.flavorText: It crosses its knifelike arms in front of its chest when it cries. It can compose melodies ad lib.

403.name: Shinx
403.flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity by contracting its muscles. Excited trembling is a sign that Shinx is generating a tremendous amount of electricity.

404.name: Luxio
404.flavorText: By joining its tail with that of another Luxio, this Pokémon can receive some of the other Luxio’s electricity and power up its own electric blasts.

405.name: Luxray
405.flavorText: Luxray can see through solid objects. It will instantly spot prey trying to hide behind walls, even if the walls are thick.

406.name: Budew
406.flavorText: The pollen it releases contains poison. If this Pokémon is raised on clean water, the poison’s toxicity is increased.

407.name: Roserade
407.flavorText: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

408.name: Cranidos
408.flavorText: A primeval Pokémon, it possesses a hard and sturdy skull, lacking any intelligence within.

409.name: Rampardos
409.flavorText: In ancient times, people would dig up fossils of this Pokémon and use its skull, which is harder than steel, to make helmets.

410.name: Shieldon
410.flavorText: A mild-mannered, herbivorous Pokémon, it used its face to dig up tree roots to eat. The skin on its face was plenty tough.

411.name: Bastiodon
411.flavorText: The bones of its face are huge and hard, so they were mistaken for its spine until after this Pokémon was successfully restored.

412.name: Burmy
412.flavorText: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

413.name: Wormadam-plant
413.flavorText: Its appearance changes depending on where it evolved. The materials on hand become a part of its body.

414.name: Mothim
414.flavorText: It loves the honey of flowers and steals honey collected by Combee.

415.name: Combee
415.flavorText: The members of the trio spend all their time together. Each one has a slightly different taste in nectar.

416.name: Vespiquen
416.flavorText: It skillfully commands its grubs in battles with its enemies. The grubs are willing to risk their lives to defend Vespiquen.

417.name: Pachirisu
417.flavorText: It makes fur balls that crackle with static electricity. It stores them with berries in tree holes.

418.name: Buizel
418.flavorText: It swims by rotating its two tails like a screw. When it dives, its flotation sac collapses.

419.name: Floatzel
419.flavorText: It floats using its well-developed flotation sac. It assists in the rescues of drowning people.

420.name: Cherubi
420.flavorText: It nimbly dashes about to avoid getting pecked by bird Pokémon that would love to make off with its small, nutrient-rich storage ball.

421.name: Cherrim
421.flavorText: As a bud, it barely moves. It sits still, placidly waiting for sunlight to appear.

422.name: Shellos
422.flavorText: This Pokémon’s habitat shapes its physique. According to some theories, life in warm ocean waters causes this variation to develop.

423.name: Gastrodon
423.flavorText: Its search for food sometimes leads it onto land, where it leaves behind a sticky trail of slime as it passes through.

424.name: Ambipom
424.flavorText: In their search for comfortable trees, they get into territorial disputes with groups of Passimian. They win about half the time.

425.name: Drifloon
425.flavorText: Perhaps seeking company, it approaches children. However, it often quickly runs away again when the children play too roughly with it.

426.name: Drifblim
426.flavorText: Some say this Pokémon is a collection of souls burdened with regrets, silently drifting through the dusk.

427.name: Buneary
427.flavorText: If both of Buneary’s ears are rolled up, something is wrong with its body or mind. It’s a sure sign the Pokémon is in need of care.

428.name: Lopunny
428.flavorText: Lopunny is constantly monitoring its surroundings. If danger approaches, this Pokémon responds with superdestructive kicks.

429.name: Mismagius
429.flavorText: Feared for its wrath and the curses it spreads, this Pokémon will also, on a whim, cast spells that help people.

430.name: Honchkrow
430.flavorText: It will absolutely not forgive failure from or betrayal by its goons. It has no choice in this if it wants to maintain the order of the flock.

431.name: Glameow
431.flavorText: It claws if displeased and purrs when affectionate. Its fickleness is very popular among some.

432.name: Purugly
432.flavorText: It would claim another Pokémon’s nest as its own if it finds a nest sufficiently comfortable.

433.name: Chingling
433.flavorText: Each time it hops, it makes a ringing sound. It deafens foes by emitting high-frequency cries.

434.name: Stunky
434.flavorText: From its rear, it sprays a foul-smelling liquid at opponents. It aims for their faces, and it can hit them from over 16 feet away.

435.name: Skuntank
435.flavorText: In its belly, it reserves stinky fluid that it shoots from its tail during battle. As this Pokémon’s diet varies, so does the stench of its fluid.

436.name: Bronzor
436.flavorText: It appears in ancient ruins. The pattern on its body doesn’t come from any culture in the Galar region, so it remains shrouded in mystery.

437.name: Bronzong
437.flavorText: Some believe it to be a deity that summons rain clouds. When angered, it lets out a warning cry that rings out like the tolling of a bell.

438.name: Bonsly
438.flavorText: It expels both sweat and tears from its eyes. The sweat is a little salty, while the tears have a slight bitterness.

439.name: Mime-jr
439.flavorText: It mimics everyone it sees, but it puts extra effort into copying the graceful dance steps of Mr. Rime as practice.

440.name: Happiny
440.flavorText: Mimicking Chansey, Happiny will place an egg- shaped stone in its belly pouch. Happiny will treasure this stone.

441.name: Chatot
441.flavorText: It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it’s one of them. This way they won’t attack it.

442.name: Spiritomb
442.flavorText: Exactly 108 spirits gathered to become this Pokémon. Apparently there are some ill-natured spirits in the mix.

443.name: Gible
443.flavorText: Gible prefers to stay in narrow holes in the sides of caves heated by geothermal energy. This way, Gible can stay warm even during a blizzard.

444.name: Gabite
444.flavorText: This Pokémon emits ultrasonic waves from a protrusion on either side of its head to probe pitch-dark caves.

445.name: Garchomp
445.flavorText: Garchomp makes its home in volcanic mountains. It flies through the sky as fast as a jet airplane, hunting down as much prey as it can.

446.name: Munchlax
446.flavorText: Stuffing itself with vast amounts of food is its only concern. Whether the food is rotten or fresh, yummy or tasteless—it does not care.

447.name: Riolu
447.flavorText: It’s exceedingly energetic, with enough stamina to keep running all through the night. Taking it for walks can be a challenging experience.

448.name: Lucario
448.flavorText: It controls waves known as auras, which are powerful enough to pulverize huge rocks. It uses these waves to take down its prey.

449.name: Hippopotas
449.flavorText: It moves through the sands with its mouth open, swallowing sand along with its prey. It gets rid of the sand by spouting it from its nose.

450.name: Hippowdon
450.flavorText: Stones can get stuck in the ports on their bodies. Dwebble help dislodge such stones, so Hippowdon look after these Pokémon.

451.name: Skorupi
451.flavorText: After burrowing into the sand, it waits patiently for prey to come near. This Pokémon and Sizzlipede share common descent.

452.name: Drapion
452.flavorText: Its poison is potent, but it rarely sees use. This Pokémon prefers to use physical force instead, going on rampages with its car-crushing strength.

453.name: Croagunk
453.flavorText: It makes frightening noises with its poison-filled cheek sacs. When opponents flinch, Croagunk hits them with a poison jab.

454.name: Toxicroak
454.flavorText: It bounces toward opponents and gouges them with poisonous claws. No more than a scratch is needed to knock out its adversaries.

455.name: Carnivine
455.flavorText: It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

456.name: Finneon
456.flavorText: When night falls, their pink patterns begin to shine. They’re popular with divers, so there are resorts that feed them to keep them close.

457.name: Lumineon
457.flavorText: Deep down at the bottom of the ocean, prey is scarce. Lumineon get into fierce disputes with Lanturn over food.

458.name: Mantyke
458.flavorText: Mantyke living in Galar seem to be somewhat sluggish. The colder waters of the seas in this region may be the cause.

459.name: Snover
459.flavorText: It lives on snowy mountains. It sinks its legs into the snow to absorb water and keep its own temperature down.

460.name: Abomasnow
460.flavorText: If it sees any packs of Darumaka going after Snover, it chases them off, swinging its sizable arms like hammers.

461.name: Weavile
461.flavorText: They attack their quarry in packs. Prey as large as Mamoswine easily fall to the teamwork of a group of Weavile.

462.name: Magnezone
462.flavorText: Some say that Magnezone receives signals from space via the antenna on its head and that it’s being controlled by some mysterious being.

463.name: Lickilicky
463.flavorText: Lickilicky’s strange tongue can stretch to many times the length of its body. No one has figured out how Lickilicky’s tongue can stretch so far.

464.name: Rhyperior
464.flavorText: It can load up to three projectiles per arm into the holes in its hands. What launches out of those holes could be either rocks or Roggenrola.

465.name: Tangrowth
465.flavorText: Tangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases. Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.

466.name: Electivire
466.flavorText: The amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse. The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.

467.name: Magmortar
467.flavorText: When Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

468.name: Togekiss
468.flavorText: These Pokémon are never seen anywhere near conflict or turmoil. In recent times, they’ve hardly been seen at all.

469.name: Yanmega
469.flavorText: It prefers to battle by biting apart foes’ heads instantly while flying by at high speed.

470.name: Leafeon
470.flavorText: Galarians favor the distinctive aroma that drifts from this Pokémon’s leaves. There’s a popular perfume made using that scent.

471.name: Glaceon
471.flavorText: Any who become captivated by the beauty of the snowfall that Glaceon creates will be frozen before they know it.

472.name: Gliscor
472.flavorText: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

473.name: Mamoswine
473.flavorText: This Pokémon can be spotted in wall paintings from as far back as 10,000 years ago. For a while, it was thought to have gone extinct.

474.name: Porygon-z
474.flavorText: Porygon-Z had a program installed to allow it to move between dimensions, but the program also caused instability in Porygon-Z’s behavior.

475.name: Gallade
475.flavorText: True to its honorable-warrior image, it uses the blades on its elbows only in defense of something or someone.

476.name: Probopass
476.flavorText: Although it can control its units known as Mini-Noses, they sometimes get lost and don’t come back.

477.name: Dusknoir
477.flavorText: At the bidding of transmissions from the spirit world, it steals people and Pokémon away. No one knows whether it has a will of its own.

478.name: Froslass
478.flavorText: After a woman met her end on a snowy mountain, her regrets lingered on. From them, this Pokémon was born. Its favorite food is frozen souls.

479.name: Rotom
479.flavorText: One boy’s invention led to the development of many different machines that take advantage of Rotom’s unique capabilities.

480.name: Uxie
480.flavorText: Known as “The Being of Knowledge.” It is said that it can wipe out the memory of those who see its eyes.

481.name: Mesprit
481.flavorText: Known as “The Being of Emotion.” It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

482.name: Azelf
482.flavorText: Known as “The Being of Willpower.” It sleeps at the bottom of a lake to keep the world in balance.

483.name: Dialga
483.flavorText: A Pokémon spoken of in legend. It is said that time began moving when Dialga was born.

484.name: Palkia
484.flavorText: It is said to live in a gap in the spatial dimension parallel to ours. It appears in mythology.

485.name: Heatran
485.flavorText: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

486.name: Regigigas
486.flavorText: All over the world, there are legends that tell of Regigigas creating other Pokémon in its own image.

487.name: Giratina-altered
487.flavorText: This Pokémon is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours, where common knowledge is distorted and strange.

488.name: Cresselia
488.flavorText: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

489.name: Phione
489.flavorText: When the water warms, they inflate the flotation sac on their heads and drift languidly on the sea in packs.

490.name: Manaphy
490.flavorText: It is born with a wondrous power that lets it bond with any kind of Pokémon.

491.name: Darkrai
491.flavorText: It chases people and Pokémon from its territory by causing them to experience deep, nightmarish slumbers.

492.name: Shaymin-land
492.flavorText: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

493.name: Arceus
493.flavorText: According to the legends of Sinnoh, this Pokémon emerged from an egg and shaped all there is in this world.

494.name: Victini
494.flavorText: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

495.name: Snivy
495.flavorText: Being exposed to sunlight makes its movements swifter. It uses vines more adeptly than its hands.

496.name: Servine
496.flavorText: It moves along the ground as if sliding. Its swift movements befuddle its foes, and it then attacks with a vine whip.

497.name: Serperior
497.flavorText: It only gives its all against strong opponents who are not fazed by the glare from Serperior’s noble eyes.

498.name: Tepig
498.flavorText: It can deftly dodge its foe’s attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

499.name: Pignite
499.flavorText: The more it eats, the more fuel it has to make the fire in its stomach stronger. This fills it with even more power.

500.name: Emboar
500.flavorText: It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.

501.name: Oshawott
501.flavorText: The scalchop on its stomach isn’t just used for battle--it can be used to break open hard berries as well.

502.name: Dewott
502.flavorText: Strict training is how it learns its flowing double-scalchop technique.

503.name: Samurott
503.flavorText: In the time it takes a foe to blink, it can draw and sheathe the seamitars attached to its front legs.

504.name: Patrat
504.flavorText: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

505.name: Watchog
505.flavorText: Using luminescent matter, it makes its eyes and body glow and stuns attacking opponents.

506.name: Lillipup
506.flavorText: This Pokémon is courageous but also cautious. It uses the soft fur covering its face to collect information about its surroundings.

507.name: Herdier
507.flavorText: Herdier is a very smart and friendly Pokémon. So much so that there’s a theory that Herdier was the first Pokémon to partner with people.

508.name: Stoutland
508.flavorText: These Pokémon seem to enjoy living with humans. Even a Stoutland caught in the wild will warm up to people in about three days.

509.name: Purrloin
509.flavorText: It steals things from people just to amuse itself with their frustration. A rivalry exists between this Pokémon and Nickit.

510.name: Liepard
510.flavorText: Don’t be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

511.name: Pansage
511.flavorText: It shares the leaf on its head with weary-looking Pokémon. These leaves are known to relieve stress.

512.name: Simisage
512.flavorText: It attacks enemies with strikes of its thorn-covered tail. This Pokémon is wild tempered.

513.name: Pansear
513.flavorText: Very intelligent, it roasts berries before eating them. It likes to help people.

514.name: Simisear
514.flavorText: A flame burns inside its body. It scatters embers from its head and tail to sear its opponents.

515.name: Panpour
515.flavorText: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. It waters plants with it using its tail.

516.name: Simipour
516.flavorText: The high-pressure water expelled from its tail is so powerful, it can destroy a concrete wall.

517.name: Munna
517.flavorText: Late at night, it appears beside people’s pillows. As it feeds on dreams, the patterns on its body give off a faint glow.

518.name: Musharna
518.flavorText: When dark mists emanate from its body, don’t get too near. If you do, your nightmares will become reality.

519.name: Pidove
519.flavorText: Where people go, these Pokémon follow. If you’re scattering food for them, be careful— several hundred of them can gather at once.

520.name: Tranquill
520.flavorText: It can fly moderately quickly. No matter how far it travels, it can always find its way back to its master and its nest.

521.name: Unfezant
521.flavorText: Unfezant are exceptional fliers. The females are known for their stamina, while the males outclass them in terms of speed.

522.name: Blitzle
522.flavorText: When thunderclouds cover the sky, it will appear. It can catch lightning with its mane and store the electricity.

523.name: Zebstrika
523.flavorText: When this ill-tempered Pokémon runs wild, it shoots lightning from its mane in all directions.

524.name: Roggenrola
524.flavorText: It’s as hard as steel, but apparently a long soak in water will cause it to soften a bit.

525.name: Boldore
525.flavorText: If you see its orange crystals start to glow, be wary. It’s about to fire off bursts of energy.

526.name: Gigalith
526.flavorText: This hardy Pokémon can often be found on construction sites and in mines, working alongside people and Copperajah.

527.name: Woobat
527.flavorText: While inside a cave, if you look up and see lots of heart-shaped marks lining the walls, it’s evidence that Woobat live there.

528.name: Swoobat
528.flavorText: Emitting powerful sound waves tires it out. Afterward, it won’t be able to fly for a little while.

529.name: Drilbur
529.flavorText: It brings its claws together and whirls around at high speed before rushing toward its prey.

530.name: Excadrill
530.flavorText: It’s not uncommon for tunnels that appear to have formed naturally to actually be a result of Excadrill’s rampant digging.

531.name: Audino
531.flavorText: Audino’s sense of hearing is superb. Not even a pebble rolling along over a mile away will escape Audino’s ears.

532.name: Timburr
532.flavorText: It loves helping out with construction projects. It loves it so much that if rain causes work to halt, it swings its log around and throws a tantrum.

533.name: Gurdurr
533.flavorText: It shows off its muscles to Machoke and other Gurdurr. If it fails to measure up to the other Pokémon, it lies low for a little while.

534.name: Conkeldurr
534.flavorText: Concrete mixed by Conkeldurr is much more durable than normal concrete, even when the compositions of the two materials are the same.

535.name: Tympole
535.flavorText: Graceful ripples running across the water’s surface are a sure sign that Tympole are singing in high-pitched voices below.

536.name: Palpitoad
536.flavorText: It weakens its prey with sound waves intense enough to cause headaches, then entangles them with its sticky tongue.

537.name: Seismitoad
537.flavorText: The vibrating of the bumps all over its body causes earthquake-like tremors. Seismitoad and Croagunk are similar species.

538.name: Throh
538.flavorText: It performs throwing moves with first-rate skill. Over the course of many battles, Throh’s belt grows darker as it absorbs its wearer’s sweat.

539.name: Sawk
539.flavorText: If you see a Sawk training in the mountains in its single-minded pursuit of strength, it’s best to quietly pass by.

540.name: Sewaddle
540.flavorText: This Pokémon makes clothes for itself. It chews up leaves and sews them with sticky thread extruded from its mouth.

541.name: Swadloon
541.flavorText: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

542.name: Leavanny
542.flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves by using the sticky silk secreted from its mouth.

543.name: Venipede
543.flavorText: Venipede and Sizzlipede are similar species, but when the two meet, a huge fight ensues.

544.name: Whirlipede
544.flavorText: This Pokémon spins itself rapidly and charges into its opponents. Its top speed is just over 60 mph.

545.name: Scolipede
545.flavorText: Scolipede latches on to its prey with the claws on its neck before slamming them into the ground and jabbing them with its claws’ toxic spikes.

546.name: Cottonee
