546.flavorText: It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

547.name: Whimsicott
547.flavorText: It scatters cotton all over the place as a prank. If it gets wet, it’ll become too heavy to move and have no choice but to answer for its mischief.

548.name: Petilil
548.flavorText: Petilil appears around sources of clean water. Boiling leaves from this Pokémon’s head results in a liquid that’s sometimes used as a bug repellent.

549.name: Lilligant
549.flavorText: It’s believed that even first-rate gardeners have a hard time getting the flower on a Lilligant’s head to bloom.

550.name: Basculin-red-striped
550.flavorText: Anglers love the fight this Pokémon puts up on the hook. And there are always more to catch— many people release them into lakes illicitly.

551.name: Sandile
551.flavorText: The desert gets cold at night, so when the sun sets, this Pokémon burrows deep into the sand and sleeps until sunrise.

552.name: Krokorok
552.flavorText: Krokorok has specialized eyes that enable it to see in the dark. This ability lets Krokorok hunt in the dead of night without getting lost.

553.name: Krookodile
553.flavorText: This Pokémon is known as the Bully of the Sands. Krookodile’s mighty jaws can bite through heavy plates of iron with almost no effort at all.

554.name: Darumaka
554.flavorText: It derives its power from fire burning inside its body. If the fire dwindles, this Pokémon will immediately fall asleep.

555.name: Darmanitan-standard
555.flavorText: The thick arms of this hot-blooded Pokémon can deliver punches capable of obliterating a dump truck.

556.name: Maractus
556.flavorText: With noises that could be mistaken for the rattles of maracas, it creates an upbeat rhythm, startling bird Pokémon and making them fly off in a hurry.

557.name: Dwebble
557.flavorText: When it finds a stone appealing, it creates a hole inside it and uses it as its home. This Pokémon is the natural enemy of Roggenrola and Rolycoly.

558.name: Crustle
558.flavorText: This highly territorial Pokémon prefers dry climates. It won’t come out of its boulder on rainy days.

559.name: Scraggy
559.flavorText: If it locks eyes with you, watch out! Nothing and no one is safe from the reckless headbutts of this troublesome Pokémon.

560.name: Scrafty
560.flavorText: As halfhearted as this Pokémon’s kicks may seem, they pack enough power to shatter Conkeldurr’s concrete pillars.

561.name: Sigilyph
561.flavorText: Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians’ emissaries.

562.name: Yamask
562.flavorText: It wanders through ruins by night, carrying a mask that’s said to have been the face it had when it was still human.

563.name: Cofagrigus
563.flavorText: This Pokémon has a body of sparkling gold. People say it no longer remembers that it was once human.

564.name: Tirtouga
564.flavorText: This Pokémon inhabited ancient seas. Although it can only crawl, it still comes up onto land in search of prey.

565.name: Carracosta
565.flavorText: Carracosta completely devours its prey—bones, shells, and all. Because of this, Carracosta’s own shell grows thick and sturdy.

566.name: Archen
566.flavorText: This Pokémon was successfully restored from a fossil. As research suggested, Archen is unable to fly. But it’s very good at jumping.

567.name: Archeops
567.flavorText: It needs a running start to take off. If Archeops wants to fly, it first needs to run nearly 25 mph, building speed over a course of about 2.5 miles.

568.name: Trubbish
568.flavorText: Its favorite places are unsanitary ones. If you leave trash lying around, you could even find one of these Pokémon living in your room.

569.name: Garbodor
569.flavorText: This Pokémon eats trash, which turns into poison inside its body. The main component of the poison depends on what sort of trash was eaten.

570.name: Zorua
570.flavorText: Zorua is a timid Pokémon. This disposition seems to be what led to the development of Zorua’s ability to take on the forms of other creatures.

571.name: Zoroark
571.flavorText: This Pokémon cares deeply about others of its kind, and it will conjure terrifying illusions to keep its den and pack safe.

572.name: Minccino
572.flavorText: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

573.name: Cinccino
573.flavorText: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won’t tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

574.name: Gothita
574.flavorText: Though they’re still only babies, there’s psychic power stored in their ribbonlike feelers, and sometimes they use that power to fight.

575.name: Gothorita
575.flavorText: It’s said that when stars shine in the night sky, this Pokémon will spirit away sleeping children. Some call it the Witch of Punishment.

576.name: Gothitelle
576.flavorText: It has tremendous psychic power, but it dislikes conflict. It’s also able to predict the future based on the movement of the stars.

577.name: Solosis
577.flavorText: It communicates with others telepathically. Its body is encapsulated in liquid, but if it takes a heavy blow, the liquid will leak out.

578.name: Duosion
578.flavorText: Its psychic power can supposedly cover a range of more than half a mile—but only if its two brains can agree with each other.

579.name: Reuniclus
579.flavorText: While it could use its psychic abilities in battle, this Pokémon prefers to swing its powerful arms around to beat opponents into submission.

580.name: Ducklett
580.flavorText: When attacked, it uses its feathers to splash water, escaping under cover of the spray.

581.name: Swanna
581.flavorText: Despite their elegant appearance, they can flap their wings strongly and fly for thousands of miles.

582.name: Vanillite
582.flavorText: Unable to survive in hot areas, it makes itself comfortable by breathing out air cold enough to cause snow. It burrows into the snow to sleep.

583.name: Vanillish
583.flavorText: By drinking pure water, it grows its icy body. This Pokémon can be hard to find on days with warm, sunny weather.

584.name: Vanilluxe
584.flavorText: When its anger reaches a breaking point, this Pokémon unleashes a fierce blizzard that freezes every creature around it, be they friend or foe.

585.name: Deerling
585.flavorText: Their coloring changes according to the seasons and can be slightly affected by the temperature and humidity as well.

586.name: Sawsbuck
586.flavorText: They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck’s horns.

587.name: Emolga
587.flavorText: As Emolga flutters through the air, it crackles with electricity. This Pokémon is cute, but it can cause a lot of trouble.

588.name: Karrablast
588.flavorText: Its strange physiology reacts to electrical energy in interesting ways. The presence of a Shelmet will cause this Pokémon to evolve.

589.name: Escavalier
589.flavorText: They use shells they’ve stolen from Shelmet to arm and protect themselves. They’re very popular Pokémon in the Galar region.

590.name: Foongus
590.flavorText: No one knows what the Poké Ball–like pattern on Foongus means or why Foongus has it.

591.name: Amoonguss
591.flavorText: This Pokémon puffs poisonous spores at its foes. If the spores aren’t washed off quickly, they’ll grow into mushrooms wherever they land.

592.name: Frillish-male
592.flavorText: It envelops its prey in its veillike arms and draws it down to the deeps, five miles below the ocean’s surface.

593.name: Jellicent-male
593.flavorText: Most of this Pokémon’s body composition is identical to sea water. It makes sunken ships its lair.

594.name: Alomomola
594.flavorText: Fishermen take them along on long voyages, because if you have an Alomomola with you, there’ll be no need for a doctor or medicine.

595.name: Joltik
595.flavorText: Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon. It’s soaking up static electricity because it can’t produce a charge on its own.

596.name: Galvantula
596.flavorText: It launches electrified fur from its abdomen as its means of attack. Opponents hit by the fur could be in for three full days and nights of paralysis.

597.name: Ferroseed
597.flavorText: It defends itself by launching spikes, but its aim isn’t very good at first. Only after a lot of practice will it improve.

598.name: Ferrothorn
598.flavorText: This Pokémon scrapes its spikes across rocks, and then uses the tips of its feelers to absorb the nutrients it finds within the stone.

599.name: Klink
599.flavorText: The two minigears that compose this Pokémon are closer than twins. They mesh well only with each other.

600.name: Klang
600.flavorText: When Klang goes all out, the minigear links up perfectly with the outer part of the big gear, and this Pokémon’s rotation speed increases sharply.

601.name: Klinklang
601.flavorText: From its spikes, it launches powerful blasts of electricity. Its red core contains an enormous amount of energy.

602.name: Tynamo
602.flavorText: While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

603.name: Eelektrik
603.flavorText: It wraps itself around its prey and paralyzes it with electricity from the round spots on its sides. Then it chomps.

604.name: Eelektross
604.flavorText: With their sucker mouths, they suck in prey. Then they use their fangs to shock the prey with electricity.

605.name: Elgyem
605.flavorText: If this Pokémon stands near a TV, strange scenery will appear on the screen. That scenery is said to be from its home.

606.name: Beheeyem
606.flavorText: Whenever a Beheeyem visits a farm, a Dubwool mysteriously disappears.

607.name: Litwick
607.flavorText: The flame on its head keeps its body slightly warm. This Pokémon takes lost children by the hand to guide them to the spirit world.

608.name: Lampent
608.flavorText: This Pokémon appears just before someone passes away, so it’s feared as an emissary of death.

609.name: Chandelure
609.flavorText: This Pokémon haunts dilapidated mansions. It sways its arms to hypnotize opponents with the ominous dancing of its flames.

610.name: Axew
610.flavorText: These Pokémon nest in the ground and use their tusks to crush hard berries. Crushing berries is also how they test each other’s strength.

611.name: Fraxure
611.flavorText: After battle, this Pokémon carefully sharpens its tusks on river rocks. It needs to take care of its tusks—if one breaks, it will never grow back.

612.name: Haxorus
612.flavorText: Its resilient tusks are its pride and joy. It licks up dirt to take in the minerals it needs to keep its tusks in top condition.

613.name: Cubchoo
613.flavorText: When this Pokémon is in good health, its snot becomes thicker and stickier. It will smear its snot on anyone it doesn’t like.

614.name: Beartic
614.flavorText: It swims through frigid seas, searching for prey. From its frozen breath, it forms icy fangs that are harder than steel.

615.name: Cryogonal
615.flavorText: With its icy chains, Cryogonal freezes those it encounters. It then takes its victims away to somewhere unknown.

616.name: Shelmet
616.flavorText: When attacked, it tightly shuts the lid of its shell. This reaction fails to protect it from Karrablast, however, because they can still get into the shell.

617.name: Accelgor
617.flavorText: It moves with blinding speed and lobs poison at foes. Featuring Accelgor as a main character is a surefire way to make a movie or comic popular.

618.name: Stunfisk
618.flavorText: Thanks to bacteria that lived in the mud flats with it, this Pokémon developed the organs it uses to generate electricity.

619.name: Mienfoo
619.flavorText: In one minute, a well-trained Mienfoo can chop with its arms more than 100 times.

620.name: Mienshao
620.flavorText: When Mienshao comes across a truly challenging opponent, it will lighten itself by biting off the fur on its arms.

621.name: Druddigon
621.flavorText: Druddigon lives in caves, but it never skips sunbathing—it won’t be able to move if its body gets too cold.

622.name: Golett
622.flavorText: They were sculpted from clay in ancient times. No one knows why, but some of them are driven to continually line up boulders.

623.name: Golurk
623.flavorText: Artillery platforms built into the walls of ancient castles served as perches from which Golurk could fire energy beams.

624.name: Pawniard
624.flavorText: It uses river stones to maintain the cutting edges of the blades covering its body. These sharpened blades allow it to bring down opponents.

625.name: Bisharp
625.flavorText: It’s accompanied by a large retinue of Pawniard. Bisharp keeps a keen eye on its minions, ensuring none of them even think of double-crossing it.

626.name: Bouffalant
626.flavorText: These Pokémon can crush a car with no more than a headbutt. Bouffalant with more hair on their heads hold higher positions within the herd.

627.name: Rufflet
627.flavorText: If it spies a strong Pokémon, Rufflet can’t resist challenging it to a battle. But if Rufflet loses, it starts bawling.

628.name: Braviary
628.flavorText: Known for its bravery and pride, this majestic Pokémon is often seen as a motif for various kinds of emblems.

629.name: Vullaby
629.flavorText: It wears a bone to protect its rear. It often squabbles with others of its kind over particularly comfy bones.

630.name: Mandibuzz
630.flavorText: Although it’s a bit of a ruffian, this Pokémon will take lost Vullaby under its wing and care for them till they’re ready to leave the nest.

631.name: Heatmor
631.flavorText: There’s a hole in its tail that allows it to draw in the air it needs to keep its fire burning. If the hole gets blocked, this Pokémon will fall ill.

632.name: Durant
632.flavorText: They lay their eggs deep inside their nests. When attacked by Heatmor, they retaliate using their massive mandibles.

633.name: Deino
633.flavorText: When it encounters something, its first urge is usually to bite it. If it likes what it tastes, it will commit the associated scent to memory.

634.name: Zweilous
634.flavorText: While hunting for prey, Zweilous wanders its territory, its two heads often bickering over which way to go.

635.name: Hydreigon
635.flavorText: There are a slew of stories about villages that were destroyed by Hydreigon. It bites anything that moves.

636.name: Larvesta
636.flavorText: The people of ancient times believed that Larvesta fell from the sun.

637.name: Volcarona
637.flavorText: Volcarona scatters burning scales. Some say it does this to start fires. Others say it’s trying to rescue those that suffer in the cold.

638.name: Cobalion
638.flavorText: This Pokémon appears in a legend alongside Terrakion and Virizion, fighting against humans in defense of the Unova region’s Pokémon.

639.name: Terrakion
639.flavorText: It has phenomenal power. It will mercilessly crush anyone or anything that bullies small Pokémon.

640.name: Virizion
640.flavorText: A legend tells of this Pokémon working together with Cobalion and Terrakion to protect the Pokémon of the Unova region.

641.name: Tornadus-incarnate
641.flavorText: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

642.name: Thundurus-incarnate
642.flavorText: The spikes on its tail discharge immense bolts of lightning. It flies around the Unova region firing off lightning bolts.

643.name: Reshiram
643.flavorText: Flames spew from its tail as it flies through the sky like a jet airplane. It’s said that this Pokémon will scorch the world.

644.name: Zekrom
644.flavorText: When the interior part of its tail spins like a motor, Zekrom can generate many bolts of lightning to blast its surroundings.

645.name: Landorus-incarnate
645.flavorText: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.”

646.name: Kyurem
646.flavorText: Dwelling within it is a power even greater than that of Reshiram or Zekrom, but the extreme cold keeps that power bound.

647.name: Keldeo-ordinary
647.flavorText: Cobalion, Terrakion, and Virizion taught this Pokémon how to fight. It dashes across the world, seeking more opportunities to further its training.

648.name: Meloetta-aria
648.flavorText: The melodies sung by Meloetta have the power to make Pokémon that hear them happy or sad.

649.name: Genesect
649.flavorText: This ancient bug Pokémon was altered by Team Plasma. They upgraded the cannon on its back.

650.name: Chespin
650.flavorText: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

651.name: Quilladin
651.flavorText: It relies on its sturdy shell to deflect predators’ attacks. It counterattacks with its sharp quills.

652.name: Chesnaught
652.flavorText: Its Tackle is forceful enough to flip a 50-ton tank. It shields its allies from danger with its own body.

653.name: Fennekin
653.flavorText: Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.

654.name: Braixen
654.flavorText: It has a twig stuck in its tail. With friction from its tail fur, it sets the twig on fire and launches into battle.

655.name: Delphox
655.flavorText: It gazes into the flame at the tip of its branch to achieve a focused state, which allows it to see into the future.

656.name: Froakie
656.flavorText: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

657.name: Frogadier
657.flavorText: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

658.name: Greninja
658.flavorText: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two.

659.name: Bunnelby
659.flavorText: It excels at digging holes. Using its ears, it can dig a nest 33 feet deep in one night.

660.name: Diggersby
660.flavorText: With power equal to an excavator, it can dig through dense bedrock. It’s a huge help during tunnel construction.

661.name: Fletchling
661.flavorText: Its melodious cries are actually warnings. Fletchling will mercilessly peck at anything that enters its territory.

662.name: Fletchinder
662.flavorText: Fletchinder launches embers into the den of its prey. When the prey comes leaping out, Fletchinder’s sharp talons finish it off.

663.name: Talonflame
663.flavorText: Talonflame mainly preys upon other bird Pokémon. To intimidate opponents, it sends embers spewing from gaps between its feathers.

664.name: Scatterbug
664.flavorText: When under attack from bird Pokémon, it spews a poisonous black powder that causes paralysis on contact.

665.name: Spewpa
665.flavorText: It lives hidden within thicket shadows. When predators attack, it quickly bristles the fur covering its body in an effort to threaten them.

666.name: Vivillon
666.flavorText: Vivillon with many different patterns are found all over the world. These patterns are affected by the climate of their habitat.

667.name: Litleo
667.flavorText: When they’re young, they live with a pride. Once they’re able to hunt prey on their own, they’re kicked out and have to make their own way.

668.name: Pyroar-male
668.flavorText: The males are usually lazy, but when attacked by a strong foe, a male will protect its friends with no regard for its own safety.

669.name: Flabebe
669.flavorText: It’s not safe without the power of a flower, but it will keep traveling around until it finds one with the color and shape it wants.

670.name: Floette
670.flavorText: It raises flowers and uses them as weapons. The more gorgeous the blossom, the more power it contains.

671.name: Florges
671.flavorText: It controls the flowers it grows. The petal blizzards that Florges triggers are overwhelming in their beauty and power.

672.name: Skiddo
672.flavorText: Thought to be one of the first Pokémon to live in harmony with humans, it has a placid disposition.

673.name: Gogoat
673.flavorText: It can tell how its Trainer is feeling by subtle shifts in the grip on its horns. This empathic sense lets them run as if one being.

674.name: Pancham
674.flavorText: It chooses a Pangoro as its master and then imitates its master’s actions. This is how it learns to battle and hunt for prey.

675.name: Pangoro
675.flavorText: This Pokémon is quick to anger, and it has no problem using its prodigious strength to get its way. It lives for duels against Obstagoon.

676.name: Furfrou
676.flavorText: There was an era when aristocrats would compete to see who could trim their Furfrou’s fur into the most exquisite style.

677.name: Espurr
677.flavorText: Though Espurr’s expression never changes, behind that blank stare is an intense struggle to contain its devastating psychic power.

678.name: Meowstic-male
678.flavorText: Revealing the eyelike patterns on the insides of its ears will unleash its psychic powers. It normally keeps the patterns hidden, however.

679.name: Honedge
679.flavorText: Honedge’s soul once belonged to a person who was killed a long time ago by the sword that makes up Honedge’s body.

680.name: Doublade
680.flavorText: Honedge evolves into twins. The two blades rub together to emit a metallic sound that unnerves opponents.

681.name: Aegislash-shield
681.flavorText: In this defensive stance, Aegislash uses its steel body and a force field of spectral power to reduce the damage of any attack.

682.name: Spritzee
682.flavorText: A scent pouch within this Pokémon’s body allows it to create various scents. A change in its diet will alter the fragrance it produces.

683.name: Aromatisse
683.flavorText: The scent that constantly emits from its fur is so powerful that this Pokémon’s companions will eventually lose their sense of smell.

684.name: Swirlix
684.flavorText: It eats its own weight in sugar every day. If it doesn’t get enough sugar, it becomes incredibly grumpy.

685.name: Slurpuff
685.flavorText: By taking in a person’s scent, it can sniff out their mental and physical condition. It’s hoped that this skill will have many medical applications.

686.name: Inkay
686.flavorText: It spins while making its luminescent spots flash. These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light.

687.name: Malamar
687.flavorText: Gazing at its luminescent spots will quickly induce a hypnotic state, putting the observer under Malamar’s control.

688.name: Binacle
688.flavorText: After two Binacle find a suitably sized rock, they adhere themselves to it and live together. They cooperate to gather food during high tide.

689.name: Barbaracle
689.flavorText: Seven Binacle come together to form one Barbaracle. The Binacle that serves as the head gives orders to those serving as the limbs.

690.name: Skrelp
690.flavorText: It drifts in the ocean, blending in with floating seaweed. When other Pokémon come to feast on the seaweed, Skrelp feasts on them instead.

691.name: Dragalge
691.flavorText: Dragalge uses a poisonous liquid capable of corroding metal to send tankers that enter its territory to the bottom of the sea.

692.name: Clauncher
692.flavorText: Clauncher’s claws can fall off during battle, but they’ll regenerate. The meat inside the claws is popular as a delicacy in Galar.

693.name: Clawitzer
693.flavorText: After using the feelers on its oversized claw to detect the location of prey, Clawitzer launches a cannonball of water at its target.

694.name: Helioptile
694.flavorText: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon’s electric moves.

695.name: Heliolisk
695.flavorText: A now-vanished desert culture treasured these Pokémon. Appropriately, when Heliolisk came to the Galar region, treasure came with them.

696.name: Tyrunt
696.flavorText: This is an ancient Pokémon, revived in modern times. It has a violent disposition, and it’ll tear apart anything it gets between its hefty jaws.

697.name: Tyrantrum
697.flavorText: This Pokémon is from about 100,000,000 years ago. It has the presence of a king, vicious but magnificent.

698.name: Amaura
698.flavorText: This Pokémon was successfully restored from a fossil. In the past, it lived with others of its kind in cold lands where there were fewer predators.

699.name: Aurorus
699.flavorText: Aurorus was restored from a fossil. It’s said that when this Pokémon howls, auroras appear in the night sky.

700.name: Sylveon
700.flavorText: By releasing enmity-erasing waves from its ribbonlike feelers, Sylveon stops any conflict.

701.name: Hawlucha
701.flavorText: It drives its opponents to exhaustion with its agile maneuvers, then ends the fight with a flashy finishing move.

702.name: Dedenne
702.flavorText: A Dedenne’s whiskers pick up electrical waves other Dedenne send out. These Pokémon share locations of food or electricity with one another.

703.name: Carbink
703.flavorText: When beset by attackers, Carbink wipes them all out by firing high-energy beams from the gems embedded in its body.

704.name: Goomy
704.flavorText: Because most of its body is water, it will dry up if the weather becomes too arid. It’s considered the weakest dragon Pokémon.

705.name: Sliggoo
705.flavorText: Although this Pokémon isn’t very strong, its body is coated in a caustic slime that can melt through anything, so predators steer clear of it.

706.name: Goodra
706.flavorText: Sometimes it misunderstands instructions and appears dazed or bewildered. Many Trainers don’t mind, finding this behavior to be adorable.

707.name: Klefki
707.flavorText: This Pokémon is constantly collecting keys. Entrust a Klefki with important keys, and the Pokémon will protect them no matter what.

708.name: Phantump
708.flavorText: After a lost child perished in the forest, their spirit possessed a tree stump, causing the spirit’s rebirth as this Pokémon.

709.name: Trevenant
709.flavorText: People fear it due to a belief that it devours any who try to cut down trees in its forest, but to the Pokémon it shares its woods with, it’s kind.

710.name: Pumpkaboo-average
710.flavorText: Spirits that wander this world are placed into Pumpkaboo’s body. They’re then moved on to the afterlife.

711.name: Gourgeist-average
711.flavorText: Eerie cries emanate from its body in the dead of night. The sounds are said to be the wails of spirits who are suffering in the afterlife.

712.name: Bergmite
712.flavorText: They chill the air around them to −150 degrees Fahrenheit, freezing the water in the air into ice that they use as armor.

713.name: Avalugg
713.flavorText: At high latitudes, this Pokémon can be found with clusters of Bergmite on its back as it swims among the icebergs.

714.name: Noibat
714.flavorText: After nightfall, they emerge from the caves they nest in during the day. Using their ultrasonic waves, they go on the hunt for ripened fruit.

715.name: Noivern
715.flavorText: Aggressive and cruel, this Pokémon will ruthlessly torment enemies that are helpless in the dark.

716.name: Xerneas
716.flavorText: Legends say it can share eternal life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree before its revival.

717.name: Yveltal
717.flavorText: When this legendary Pokémon’s wings and tail feathers spread wide and glow red, it absorbs the life force of living creatures.

718.name: Zygarde-50
718.flavorText: This is Zygarde’s form when about half of its pieces have been assembled. It plays the role of monitoring the ecosystem.

719.name: Diancie
719.flavorText: A sudden transformation of Carbink, its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.

720.name: Hoopa
720.flavorText: This troublemaker sends anything and everything to faraway places using its loop, which can warp space.

721.name: Volcanion
721.flavorText: It lets out billows of steam and disappears into the dense fog. It’s said to live in mountains where humans do not tread.

722.name: Rowlet
722.flavorText: At a distance, it launches its sharp feathers while flying about. If the enemy gets too close, Rowlet switches tactics and delivers vicious kicks.

723.name: Dartrix
723.flavorText: It throws one knifelike feather after another at its enemies, and each one precisely strikes a weak point. These feathers are known as blade quills.

724.name: Decidueye
724.flavorText: As if wielding a bow, it launches the arrow quills hidden among the feathers of its wings. Decidueye’s shots never miss.

725.name: Litten
725.flavorText: It spends even the smallest amount of downtime grooming its fur with its tongue. Loose fur gathers in its stomach and serves as fuel for fiery moves.

726.name: Torracat
726.flavorText: In the midst of battle, the fire pouch on Torracat’s neck rings like a bell and produces stronger flames than usual.

727.name: Incineroar
727.flavorText: It excels at violent, no-holds-barred battles. The temperature of the flames that issue from its navel exceeds 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

728.name: Popplio
728.flavorText: It creates special bouncy balloons to send at its opponents. When the balloons pop, the sheer force of their bursting is what deals damage.

729.name: Brionne
729.flavorText: As if dancing, it artfully dodges the attacks of its enemies. All the while, it’s busy forming a bunch of balloons to overwhelm its foes.

730.name: Primarina
730.flavorText: With its mouth, it makes sonic waves that sound like beautiful singing. It uses the sonic waves to control its water balloons.

731.name: Pikipek
731.flavorText: It pecks at trees with its hard beak. You can get some idea of its mood or condition from the rhythm of its pecking.

732.name: Trumbeak
732.flavorText: It can bend the tip of its beak to produce over a hundred different cries at will.

733.name: Toucannon
733.flavorText: They smack beaks with others of their kind to communicate. The strength and number of hits tell each other how they feel.

734.name: Yungoos
734.flavorText: Its stomach takes up most of its long torso. It’s a big eater, so the amount Trainers have to spend on its food is no laughing matter.

735.name: Gumshoos
735.flavorText: Although it wasn’t originally found in Alola, this Pokémon was brought over a long time ago when there was a huge Rattata outbreak.

736.name: Grubbin
736.flavorText: Its natural enemies, like Rookidee, may flee rather than risk getting caught in its large mandibles that can snap thick tree branches.

737.name: Charjabug
737.flavorText: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

738.name: Vikavolt
738.flavorText: It builds up electricity in its abdomen, focuses it through its jaws, and then fires the electricity off in concentrated beams.

739.name: Crabrawler
739.flavorText: Its hard pincers are well suited to both offense and defense. Fights between two Crabrawler are like boxing matches.

740.name: Crabominable
740.flavorText: It stores coldness in its pincers and pummels its foes. It can even smash thick walls of ice to bits!

741.name: Oricorio-baile
741.flavorText: It wins the hearts of its enemies with its passionate dancing and then uses the opening it creates to burn them up with blazing flames.

742.name: Cutiefly
742.flavorText: Nectar and pollen are its favorite fare. You can find Cutiefly hovering around Gossifleur, trying to get some of Gossifleur’s pollen.

743.name: Ribombee
743.flavorText: It makes pollen puffs from pollen and nectar. The puffs’ effects depend on the type of ingredients and how much of each one is used.

744.name: Rockruff
744.flavorText: This Pokémon can bond very strongly with its Trainer, but it also has a habit of biting. Raising a Rockruff for a long time can be challenging.

745.name: Lycanroc-midday
745.flavorText: This Lycanroc is calm and cautious. The rocks jutting from its mane are razor sharp.

746.name: Wishiwashi-solo
746.flavorText: Individually, they’re incredibly weak. It’s by gathering up into schools that they’re able to confront opponents.

747.name: Mareanie
747.flavorText: The first symptom of its sting is numbness. The next is an itching sensation so intense that it’s impossible to resist the urge to claw at your skin.

748.name: Toxapex
748.flavorText: To survive in the cold waters of Galar, this Pokémon forms a dome with its legs, enclosing its body so it can capture its own body heat.

749.name: Mudbray
749.flavorText: Loads weighing up to 50 times as much as its own body weight pose no issue for this Pokémon. It’s skilled at making use of mud.

750.name: Mudsdale
750.flavorText: Mud that hardens around a Mudsdale’s legs sets harder than stone. It’s so hard that it allows this Pokémon to scrap a truck with a single kick.

751.name: Dewpider
751.flavorText: It forms a water bubble at the rear of its body and then covers its head with it. Meeting another Dewpider means comparing water-bubble sizes.

752.name: Araquanid
752.flavorText: It launches water bubbles with its legs, drowning prey within the bubbles. This Pokémon can then take its time to savor its meal.

753.name: Fomantis
753.flavorText: When bathed in sunlight, this Pokémon emits a pleasantly sweet scent, which causes bug Pokémon to gather around it.

754.name: Lurantis
754.flavorText: This Pokémon resembles a beautiful flower. A properly raised Lurantis will have gorgeous, brilliant colors.

755.name: Morelull
755.flavorText: Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull’s head. The caps regrow overnight.

756.name: Shiinotic
756.flavorText: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

757.name: Salandit
757.flavorText: Its venom sacs produce a fluid that this Pokémon then heats up with the flame in its tail. This process creates Salandit’s poisonous gas.

758.name: Salazzle
758.flavorText: Only female Salazzle exist. They emit a gas laden with pheromones to captivate male Salandit.

759.name: Stufful
759.flavorText: Its fluffy fur is a delight to pet, but carelessly reaching out to touch this Pokémon could result in painful retaliation.

760.name: Bewear
760.flavorText: Once it accepts you as a friend, it tries to show its affection with a hug. Letting it do that is dangerous—it could easily shatter your bones.

761.name: Bounsweet
761.flavorText: Its body gives off a sweet, fruity scent that is extremely appetizing to bird Pokémon.

762.name: Steenee
762.flavorText: As it twirls like a dancer, a sweet smell spreads out around it. Anyone who inhales the scent will feel a surge of happiness.

763.name: Tsareena
763.flavorText: This feared Pokémon has long, slender legs and a cruel heart. It shows no mercy as it stomps on its opponents.

764.name: Comfey
764.flavorText: Comfey picks flowers with its vine and decorates itself with them. For some reason, flowers won’t wither once they’re attached to a Comfey.

765.name: Oranguru
765.flavorText: With waves of its fan—made from leaves and its own fur—Oranguru skillfully gives instructions to other Pokémon.

766.name: Passimian
766.flavorText: Displaying amazing teamwork, they follow the orders of their boss as they all help out in the search for their favorite berries.

767.name: Wimpod
767.flavorText: It’s nature’s cleaner—it eats anything and everything, including garbage and rotten things. The ground near its nest is always clean.

768.name: Golisopod
768.flavorText: It will do anything to win, taking advantage of every opening and finishing opponents off with the small claws on its front legs.

769.name: Sandygast
769.flavorText: Grudges of the dead have possessed a mound of sand and become a Pokémon. Sandygast is fond of the shovel on its head.

770.name: Palossand
770.flavorText: Palossand is known as the Beach Nightmare. It pulls its prey down into the sand by controlling the sand itself, and then it sucks out their souls.

771.name: Pyukumuku
771.flavorText: It lives in warm, shallow waters. If it encounters a foe, it will spit out its internal organs as a means to punch them.

772.name: Type-null
772.flavorText: Rumor has it that the theft of top-secret research notes led to a new instance of this Pokémon being created in the Galar region.

773.name: Silvally
773.flavorText: A solid bond of trust between this Pokémon and its Trainer awakened the strength hidden within Silvally. It can change its type at will.

774.name: Minior-red-meteor
774.flavorText: It lives in the ozone layer, where it becomes food for stronger Pokémon. When it tries to run away, it falls to the ground.

775.name: Komala
775.flavorText: It stays asleep from the moment it’s born. When it falls into a deep sleep, it stops moving altogether.

776.name: Turtonator
776.flavorText: Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.

777.name: Togedemaru
777.flavorText: With the long hairs on its back, this Pokémon takes in electricity from other electric Pokémon. It stores what it absorbs in an electric sac.

778.name: Mimikyu-disguised
778.flavorText: It wears a rag fashioned into a Pikachu costume in an effort to look less scary. Unfortunately, the costume only makes it creepier.

779.name: Bruxish
779.flavorText: It burrows beneath the sand, radiating psychic power from the protuberance on its head. It waits for prey as it surveys the area.

780.name: Drampa
780.flavorText: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.

781.name: Dhelmise
781.flavorText: After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.

782.name: Jangmo-o
782.flavorText: They learn to fight by smashing their head scales together. The dueling strengthens both their skills and their spirits.

783.name: Hakamo-o
783.flavorText: The scaleless, scarred parts of its body are signs of its strength. It shows them off to defeated opponents.

784.name: Kommo-o
784.flavorText: It clatters its tail scales to unnerve opponents. This Pokémon will battle only those who stand steadfast in the face of this display.

785.name: Tapu-koko
785.flavorText: Although it’s called a guardian deity, if a person or Pokémon puts it in a bad mood, it will become a malevolent deity and attack.

786.name: Tapu-lele
786.flavorText: It heals the wounds of people and Pokémon by sprinkling them with its sparkling scales. This guardian deity is worshiped on Akala.

787.name: Tapu-bulu
787.flavorText: Although it’s called a guardian deity, it’s violent enough to crush anyone it sees as an enemy.

788.name: Tapu-fini
788.flavorText: This guardian deity of Poni Island manipulates water. Because it lives deep within a thick fog, it came to be both feared and revered.

789.name: Cosmog
789.flavorText: This Pokémon came from another universe. Its gaseous body is so light that even a gentle breeze can blow it away.

790.name: Cosmoem
790.flavorText: The absorption of starlight fuels this Pokémon’s growth. The shell that encases it is harder than any known material.

791.name: Solgaleo
791.flavorText: When light radiates from its body, this Pokémon could almost appear to be the sun. It will dispel any darkness and light up the world.

792.name: Lunala
792.flavorText: Known as the Beast That Calls the Moon, this Pokémon lives by taking in any and all light and converting it into its own energy.

793.name: Nihilego
793.flavorText: A life-form from another world, it was dubbed a UB and is thought to produce a strong neurotoxin.

794.name: Buzzwole
794.flavorText: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

795.name: Pheromosa
795.flavorText: A life-form that lives in another world, its body is thin and supple, but it also possesses great power.

796.name: Xurkitree
796.flavorText: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

797.name: Celesteela
797.flavorText: One of the dangerous UBs, high energy readings can be detected coming from both of its huge arms.

798.name: Kartana
798.flavorText: This Ultra Beast’s body, which is as thin as paper, is like a sharpened sword.

799.name: Guzzlord
799.flavorText: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

800.name: Necrozma
800.flavorText: It survives by absorbing light. After a long time spent slumbering underground, impurities accumulated within it, causing its body to darken.

801.name: Magearna
801.flavorText: It synchronizes its consciousness with others to understand their feelings. This faculty makes it useful for taking care of people.

802.name: Marshadow
802.flavorText: By slipping into the shadow of a martial arts master and copying their movements, this Pokémon learned the ultimate techniques.

803.name: Poipole
803.flavorText: This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.

804.name: Naganadel
804.flavorText: It stores hundreds of liters of poisonous liquid inside its body. It is one of the organisms known as UBs.

805.name: Stakataka
805.flavorText: It appeared from an Ultra Wormhole. Each one appears to be made up of many life-forms stacked one on top of each other.

806.name: Blacephalon
806.flavorText: It slithers toward people. Then, without warning, it triggers the explosion of its own head. It’s apparently one kind of Ultra Beast.

807.name: Zeraora
807.flavorText: It runs as fast as lightning strikes, shredding its opponents with its high-voltage claws.

808.name: Meltan
808.flavorText: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

809.name: Melmetal
809.flavorText: At the end of its life-span, Melmetal will rust and fall apart. The small shards left behind will eventually be reborn as Meltan.

810.name: Grookey
810.flavorText: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

811.name: Thwackey
811.flavorText: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

812.name: Rillaboom
812.flavorText: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.

813.name: Scorbunny
813.flavorText: A warm-up of running around gets fire energy coursing through this Pokémon’s body. Once that happens, it’s ready to fight at full power.

814.name: Raboot
814.flavorText: Its thick and fluffy fur protects it from the cold and enables it to use hotter fire moves.

815.name: Cinderace
815.flavorText: It juggles a pebble with its feet, turning it into a burning soccer ball. Its shots strike opponents hard and leave them scorched.

816.name: Sobble
816.flavorText: When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won’t be able to resist weeping.

817.name: Drizzile
817.flavorText: A clever combatant, this Pokémon battles using water balloons created with moisture secreted from its palms.

818.name: Inteleon
818.flavorText: It has many hidden capabilities, such as fingertips that can shoot water and a membrane on its back that it can use to glide through the air.

819.name: Skwovet
819.flavorText: Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.

820.name: Greedent
820.flavorText: It stashes berries in its tail—so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn’t notice the loss.

821.name: Rookidee
821.flavorText: It will bravely challenge any opponent, no matter how powerful. This Pokémon benefits from every battle—even a defeat increases its strength a bit.

822.name: Corvisquire
822.flavorText: Smart enough to use tools in battle, these Pokémon have been seen picking up rocks and flinging them or using ropes to wrap up enemies.

823.name: Corviknight
823.flavorText: This Pokémon reigns supreme in the skies of the Galar region. The black luster of its steel body could drive terror into the heart of any foe.

824.name: Blipbug
824.flavorText: A constant collector of information, this Pokémon is very smart. Very strong is what it isn’t.

825.name: Dottler
825.flavorText: It barely moves, but it’s still alive. Hiding in its shell without food or water seems to have awakened its psychic powers.

826.name: Orbeetle
826.flavorText: It’s famous for its high level of intelligence, and the large size of its brain is proof that it also possesses immense psychic power.

827.name: Nickit
827.flavorText: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

828.name: Thievul
828.flavorText: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

829.name: Gossifleur
829.flavorText: It anchors itself in the ground with its single leg, then basks in the sun. After absorbing enough sunlight, its petals spread as it blooms brilliantly.

830.name: Eldegoss
830.flavorText: The seeds attached to its cotton fluff are full of nutrients. It spreads them on the wind so that plants and other Pokémon can benefit from them.

831.name: Wooloo
831.flavorText: Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.

832.name: Dubwool
832.flavorText: Weave a carpet from its springy wool, and you end up with something closer to a trampoline. You’ll start to bounce the moment you set foot on it.

833.name: Chewtle
833.flavorText: Apparently the itch of its teething impels it to snap its jaws at anything in front of it.

834.name: Drednaw
834.flavorText: With jaws that can shear through steel rods, this highly aggressive Pokémon chomps down on its unfortunate prey.

835.name: Yamper
835.flavorText: This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.

836.name: Boltund
836.flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

837.name: Rolycoly
837.flavorText: Most of its body has the same composition as coal. Fittingly, this Pokémon was first discovered in coal mines about 400 years ago.

838.name: Carkol
838.flavorText: It forms coal inside its body. Coal dropped by this Pokémon once helped fuel the lives of people in the Galar region.

839.name: Coalossal
839.flavorText: It’s usually peaceful, but the vandalism of mines enrages it. Offenders will be incinerated with flames that reach 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

840.name: Applin
840.flavorText: It spends its entire life inside an apple. It hides from its natural enemies, bird Pokémon, by pretending it’s just an apple and nothing more.

841.name: Flapple
841.flavorText: It ate a sour apple, and that induced its evolution. In its cheeks, it stores an acid capable of causing chemical burns.

842.name: Appletun
842.flavorText: Eating a sweet apple caused its evolution. A nectarous scent wafts from its body, luring in the bug Pokémon it preys on.

843.name: Silicobra
843.flavorText: As it digs, it swallows sand and stores it in its neck pouch. The pouch can hold more than 17 pounds of sand.

844.name: Sandaconda
844.flavorText: When it contracts its body, over 220 pounds of sand sprays from its nose. If it ever runs out of sand, it becomes disheartened.

845.name: Cramorant
845.flavorText: It’s so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it’s battling midfight.

846.name: Arrokuda
846.flavorText: If it sees any movement around it, this Pokémon charges for it straightaway, leading with its sharply pointed jaw. It’s very proud of that jaw.

847.name: Barraskewda
847.flavorText: This Pokémon has a jaw that’s as sharp as a spear and as strong as steel. Apparently Barraskewda’s flesh is surprisingly tasty, too.

848.name: Toxel
848.flavorText: It stores poison in an internal poison sac and secretes that poison through its skin. If you touch this Pokémon, a tingling sensation follows.

849.name: Toxtricity-amped
849.flavorText: When this Pokémon sounds as if it’s strumming a guitar, it’s actually clawing at the protrusions on its chest to generate electricity.

850.name: Sizzlipede
850.flavorText: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

851.name: Centiskorch
851.flavorText: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

852.name: Clobbopus
852.flavorText: It’s very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

853.name: Grapploct
853.flavorText: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.

854.name: Sinistea
854.flavorText: This Pokémon is said to have been born when a lonely spirit possessed a cold, leftover cup of tea.

855.name: Polteageist
855.flavorText: This species lives in antique teapots. Most pots are forgeries, but on rare occasions, an authentic work is found.

856.name: Hatenna
856.flavorText: Via the protrusion on its head, it senses other creatures’ emotions. If you don’t have a calm disposition, it will never warm up to you.

857.name: Hattrem
857.flavorText: No matter who you are, if you bring strong emotions near this Pokémon, it will silence you violently.

858.name: Hatterene
858.flavorText: It emits psychic power strong enough to cause headaches as a deterrent to the approach of others.

859.name: Impidimp
859.flavorText: Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy.

860.name: Morgrem
860.flavorText: When it gets down on all fours as if to beg for forgiveness, it’s trying to lure opponents in so that it can stab them with its spear-like hair.

861.name: Grimmsnarl
861.flavorText: With the hair wrapped around its body helping to enhance its muscles, this Pokémon can overwhelm even Machamp.

862.name: Obstagoon
862.flavorText: Its voice is staggering in volume. Obstagoon has a tendency to take on a threatening posture and shout—this move is known as Obstruct.

863.name: Perrserker
863.flavorText: What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

864.name: Cursola
864.flavorText: Its shell is overflowing with its heightened otherworldly energy. The ectoplasm serves as protection for this Pokémon’s core spirit.

865.name: Sirfetchd
865.flavorText: Only Farfetch’d that have survived many battles can attain this evolution. When this Pokémon’s leek withers, it will retire from combat.

866.name: Mr-rime
866.flavorText: It’s highly skilled at tap-dancing. It waves its cane of ice in time with its graceful movements.

867.name: Runerigus
867.flavorText: A powerful curse was woven into an ancient painting. After absorbing the spirit of a Yamask, the painting began to move.

868.name: Milcery
868.flavorText: This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.

869.name: Alcremie
869.flavorText: When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them to berries it’s decorated with cream.

870.name: Falinks
870.flavorText: Five of them are troopers, and one is the brass. The brass’s orders are absolute.

871.name: Pincurchin
871.flavorText: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

872.name: Snom
872.flavorText: It spits out thread imbued with a frigid sort of energy and uses it to tie its body to branches, disguising itself as an icicle while it sleeps.

873.name: Frosmoth
873.flavorText: Icy scales fall from its wings like snow as it flies over fields and mountains. The temperature of its wings is less than −290 degrees Fahrenheit.

874.name: Stonjourner
874.flavorText: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun’s descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

875.name: Eiscue-ice
875.flavorText: It drifted in on the flow of ocean waters from a frigid place. It keeps its head iced constantly to make sure it stays nice and cold.

876.name: Indeedee-male
876.flavorText: It uses the horns on its head to sense the emotions of others. Males will act as valets for those they serve, looking after their every need.

877.name: Morpeko-full-belly
877.flavorText: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It’s also generating electricity.

878.name: Cufant
878.flavorText: It digs up the ground with its trunk. It’s also very strong, being able to carry loads of over five tons without any problem at all.

879.name: Copperajah
879.flavorText: They came over from another region long ago and worked together with humans. Their green skin is resistant to water.

880.name: Dracozolt
880.flavorText: In ancient times, it was unbeatable thanks to its powerful lower body, but it went extinct anyway after it depleted all its plant-based food sources.

881.name: Arctozolt
881.flavorText: The shaking of its freezing upper half is what generates its electricity. It has a hard time walking around.

882.name: Dracovish
882.flavorText: Powerful legs and jaws made it the apex predator of its time. Its own overhunting of its prey was what drove it to extinction.

883.name: Arctovish
883.flavorText: Though it’s able to capture prey by freezing its surroundings, it has trouble eating the prey afterward because its mouth is on top of its head.

884.name: Duraludon
884.flavorText: Its body resembles polished metal, and it’s both lightweight and strong. The only drawback is that it rusts easily.

885.name: Dreepy
885.flavorText: After being reborn as a ghost Pokémon, Dreepy wanders the areas it used to inhabit back when it was alive in prehistoric seas.

886.name: Drakloak
886.flavorText: It’s capable of flying faster than 120 mph. It battles alongside Dreepy and dotes on them until they successfully evolve.

887.name: Dragapult
887.flavorText: When it isn’t battling, it keeps Dreepy in the holes on its horns. Once a fight starts, it launches the Dreepy like supersonic missiles.

888.name: Zacian
888.flavorText: Known as a legendary hero, this Pokémon absorbs metal particles, transforming them into a weapon it uses to battle.

889.name: Zamazenta
889.flavorText: In times past, it worked together with a king of the people to save the Galar region. It absorbs metal that it then uses in battle.

890.name: Eternatus
890.flavorText: The core on its chest absorbs energy emanating from the lands of the Galar region. This energy is what allows Eternatus to stay active.

891.name: Kubfu
891.flavorText: Kubfu trains hard to perfect its moves. The moves it masters will determine which form it takes when it evolves.

892.name: Urshifu-single-strike
892.flavorText: This form of Urshifu is a strong believer in the one-hit KO. Its strategy is to leap in close to foes and land a devastating blow with a hardened fist.

893.name: Zarude
893.flavorText: Within dense forests, this Pokémon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It’s incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokémon of the forest fear it.

894.name: Regieleki
894.flavorText: This Pokémon is a cluster of electrical energy. It’s said that removing the rings on Regieleki’s body will unleash the Pokémon’s latent power.

895.name: Regidrago
895.flavorText: An academic theory proposes that Regidrago’s arms were once the head of an ancient dragon Pokémon. The theory remains unproven.

896.name: Glastrier
896.flavorText: Glastrier emits intense cold from its hooves. It’s also a belligerent Pokémon—anything it wants, it takes by force.

897.name: Spectrier
897.flavorText: It probes its surroundings with all its senses save one—it doesn’t use its sense of sight. Spectrier’s kicks are said to separate soul from body.

898.name: Calyrex
898.flavorText: Calyrex is a merciful Pokémon, capable of providing healing and blessings. It reigned over the Galar region in times of yore.

899.name: Wyrdeer
899.flavorText: The black orbs shine with an uncanny light when the Pokémon is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat well and is a highly useful material for winter clothing.

900.name: Kleavor
900.flavorText: A violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one's only recourse is to flee.

901.name: Ursaluna
901.flavorText: I believe it was Hisui's swampy terrain that gave Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.

902.name: Basculegion-male
902.flavorText: Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.

903.name: Sneasler
903.flavorText: Because of Sneasler's virulent poison and daunting physical prowess, no other species could hope to best it on the frozen highlands. Preferring solitude, this species does not form packs.

904.name: Overqwil
904.flavorText: Its lancelike spikes and savage temperament have earned it the nickname ”sea fiend.” It slurps up poison to nourish itself.

905.name: Enamorus-incarnate
905.flavorText: When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon's love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.

906.name: Sprigatito
906.flavorText: Its fluffy fur is similar in composition to plants. This Pokémon frequently washes its face to keep it from drying out.

907.name: Floragato
907.flavorText: Floragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.

908.name: Meowscarada
908.flavorText: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.

909.name: Fuecoco
909.flavorText: It lies on warm rocks and uses the heat absorbed by its square-shaped scales to create fire energy.

910.name: Crocalor
910.flavorText: The combination of Crocalor’s fire energy and overflowing vitality has caused an egg-shaped fireball to appear on the Pokémon’s head.

911.name: Skeledirge
911.flavorText: The fiery bird changes shape when Skeledirge sings. Rumor has it that the bird was born when the fireball on Skeledirge’s head gained a soul.

912.name: Quaxly
912.flavorText: This Pokémon migrated to Paldea from distant lands long ago. The gel secreted by its feathers repels water and grime.

913.name: Quaxwell
913.flavorText: These Pokémon constantly run through shallow waters to train their legs, then compete with each other to see which of them kicks most gracefully.

914.name: Quaquaval
914.flavorText: A single kick from a Quaquaval can send a truck rolling. This Pokémon uses its powerful legs to perform striking dances from far-off lands.

915.name: Lechonk
915.flavorText: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn’t use it for anything other than foraging.

916.name: Oinkologne-male
916.flavorText: Oinkologne is proud of its fine, glossy skin. It emits a concentrated scent from the tip of its tail.

917.name: Tarountula
917.flavorText: The ball of threads wrapped around its body is elastic enough to deflect the scythes of Scyther, this Pokémon’s natural enemy.

918.name: Spidops
918.flavorText: It clings to branches and ceilings using its threads and moves without a sound. It takes out its prey before the prey even notices it.

919.name: Nymble
919.flavorText: It has its third set of legs folded up. When it’s in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.

920.name: Lokix
920.flavorText: When it decides to fight all out, it stands on its previously folded legs to enter Showdown Mode. It neutralizes its enemies in short order.

921.name: Pawmi
921.flavorText: It has underdeveloped electric sacs on its cheeks. These sacs can produce electricity only if Pawmi rubs them furiously with the pads on its forepaws.

922.name: Pawmo
922.flavorText: When its group is attacked, Pawmo is the first to leap into battle, defeating enemies with a fighting technique that utilizes electric shocks.

923.name: Pawmot
923.flavorText: This Pokémon normally is slow to react, but once it enters battle, it will strike down its enemies with lightning-fast movements.

924.name: Tandemaus
924.flavorText: Exhibiting great teamwork, they use their incisors to cut pieces out of any material that might be useful for a nest, then make off with them.

925.name: Maushold-family-of-four
925.flavorText: The two little ones just appeared one day. The group might be a family of related Pokémon, but nobody knows for sure.

926.name: Fidough
926.flavorText: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough’s breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon’s vicinity.

927.name: Dachsbun
927.flavorText: The pleasant aroma that emanates from this Pokémon’s body helps wheat grow, so Dachsbun has been treasured by farming villages.

928.name: Smoliv
928.flavorText: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

929.name: Dolliv
929.flavorText: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

930.name: Arboliva
930.flavorText: This calm Pokémon is very compassionate. It will share its delicious, nutrient-rich oil with weakened Pokémon.

931.name: Squawkabilly-green-plumage
931.flavorText: These Pokémon prefer to live in cities. They form flocks based on the color of their feathers, and they fight over territory.

932.name: Nacli
932.flavorText: It was born in a layer of rock salt deep under the earth. This species was particularly treasured in the old days, as they would share precious salt.

933.name: Naclstack
933.flavorText: This Pokémon dry cures its prey by spraying salt over them. The curing process steals away the water in the prey’s body.

934.name: Garganacl
934.flavorText: Garganacl will rub its fingertips together and sprinkle injured Pokémon with salt. Even severe wounds will promptly heal afterward.

935.name: Charcadet
935.flavorText: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

936.name: Armarouge
936.flavorText: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

937.name: Ceruledge
937.flavorText: The fiery blades on its arms burn fiercely with the lingering resentment of a sword wielder who fell before accomplishing their goal.

938.name: Tadbulb
938.flavorText: Tadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies.

939.name: Bellibolt
939.flavorText: When this Pokémon expands and contracts its wobbly body, the belly-button dynamo in its stomach produces a huge amount of electricity.

940.name: Wattrel
940.flavorText: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

941.name: Kilowattrel
941.flavorText: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

942.name: Maschiff
942.flavorText: It always scowls in an attempt to make opponents take it seriously, but even crying children will burst into laughter when they see Maschiff’s face.

943.name: Mabosstiff
943.flavorText: This Pokémon can store energy in its large dewlap. Mabosstiff unleashes this energy all at once to blow away enemies.

944.name: Shroodle
944.flavorText: Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

945.name: Grafaiai
945.flavorText: The color of the poisonous saliva depends on what the Pokémon eats. Grafaiai covers its fingers in its saliva and draws patterns on trees in forests.

946.name: Bramblin
946.flavorText: A soul unable to move on to the afterlife was blown around by the wind until it got tangled up with dried grass and became a Pokémon.

947.name: Brambleghast
947.flavorText: It will open the branches of its head to envelop its prey. Once it absorbs all the life energy it needs, it expels the prey and discards it.

948.name: Toedscool
948.flavorText: Toedscool lives in muggy forests. The flaps that fall from its body are chewy and very delicious.

949.name: Toedscruel
949.flavorText: These Pokémon gather into groups and form colonies deep within forests. They absolutely hate it when strangers approach.

950.name: Klawf
950.flavorText: Klawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can’t remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.

951.name: Capsakid
951.flavorText: The more sunlight this Pokémon bathes in, the more spicy chemicals are produced by its body, and thus the spicier its moves become.

952.name: Scovillain
952.flavorText: The red head converts spicy chemicals into fire energy and blasts the surrounding area with a super spicy stream of flame.

953.name: Rellor
953.flavorText: This Pokémon creates a mud ball by mixing sand and dirt with psychic energy. It treasures its mud ball more than its own life.

954.name: Rabsca
954.flavorText: The body that supports the ball barely moves. Therefore, it is thought that the true body of this Pokémon is actually inside the ball.

955.name: Flittle
955.flavorText: Flittle’s toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon’s belly.

956.name: Espathra
956.flavorText: It immobilizes opponents by bathing them in psychic power from its large eyes. Despite its appearance, it has a vicious temperament.

957.name: Tinkatink
957.flavorText: It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.

958.name: Tinkatuff
958.flavorText: This Pokémon will attack groups of Pawniard and Bisharp, gathering metal from them in order to create a large and sturdy hammer.

959.name: Tinkaton
959.flavorText: This intelligent Pokémon has a very daring disposition. It knocks rocks into the sky with its hammer, aiming for flying Corviknight.

960.name: Wiglett
960.flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

961.name: Wugtrio
961.flavorText: It has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.

962.name: Bombirdier
962.flavorText: It gathers things up in an apron made from shed feathers added to the Pokémon’s chest feathers, then drops those things from high places for fun.

963.name: Finizen
963.flavorText: It likes playing with others of its kind using the water ring on its tail. It uses ultrasonic waves to sense the emotions of other living creatures.

964.name: Palafin-zero
964.flavorText: This Pokémon changes its appearance if it hears its allies calling for help. Palafin will never show anybody its moment of transformation.

965.name: Varoom
965.flavorText: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

966.name: Revavroom
966.flavorText: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders—now numbering eight—to generate energy.

967.name: Cyclizar
967.flavorText: Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

968.name: Orthworm
968.flavorText: When attacked, this Pokémon will wield the tendrils on its body like fists and pelt the opponent with a storm of punches.

969.name: Glimmet
969.flavorText: It absorbs nutrients from cave walls. The petals it wears are made of crystallized poison.

970.name: Glimmora
970.flavorText: When this Pokémon detects danger, it will open up its crystalline petals and fire beams from its conical body.

971.name: Greavard
971.flavorText: It is said that a dog Pokémon that died in the wild without ever interacting with a human was reborn as this Pokémon.

972.name: Houndstone
972.flavorText: Houndstone spends most of its time sleeping in graveyards. Among all the dog Pokémon, this one is most loyal to its master.

973.name: Flamigo
973.flavorText: This Pokémon apparently ties the base of its neck into a knot so that energy stored in its belly does not escape from its beak.

974.name: Cetoddle
974.flavorText: This species left the ocean and began living on land a very long time ago. It seems to be closely related to Wailmer.

975.name: Cetitan
975.flavorText: This Pokémon wanders around snowy, icy areas. It protects its body with powerful muscles and a thick layer of fat under its skin.

976.name: Veluza
976.flavorText: When Veluza discards unnecessary flesh, its mind becomes honed and its psychic power increases. The spare flesh has a mild but delicious flavor.

977.name: Dondozo
977.flavorText: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it’s bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

978.name: Tatsugiri-curly
978.flavorText: This is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

979.name: Annihilape
979.flavorText: When its anger rose beyond a critical point, this Pokémon gained power that is unfettered by the limits of its physical body.

980.name: Clodsire
980.flavorText: When attacked, this Pokémon will retaliate by sticking thick spines out from its body. It’s a risky move that puts everything on the line.

981.name: Farigiraf
981.flavorText: Now that the brain waves from the head and tail are synced up, the psychic power of this Pokémon is 10 times stronger than Girafarig’s.

982.name: Dudunsparce-two-segment
982.flavorText: This Pokémon uses its hard tail to make its nest by boring holes into bedrock deep underground. The nest can reach lengths of over six miles.

983.name: Kingambit
983.flavorText: Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.

984.name: Great-tusk
984.flavorText: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. The name Great Tusk was taken from a creature listed in a certain book.

985.name: Scream-tail
985.flavorText: There has been only one reported sighting of this Pokémon. It resembles a mysterious creature depicted in an old expedition journal.

986.name: Brute-bonnet
986.flavorText: It is possible that the creature listed as Brute Bonnet in a certain book could actually be this Pokémon.

987.name: Flutter-mane
987.flavorText: This Pokémon has characteristics similar to those of Flutter Mane, a creature mentioned in a certain book.

988.name: Slither-wing
988.flavorText: This mysterious Pokémon has some similarities to a creature that an old book introduced as Slither Wing.

989.name: Sandy-shocks
989.flavorText: No records exist of this Pokémon being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon’s traits match up with a creature shown in an expedition journal.

990.name: Iron-treads
990.flavorText: This Pokémon closely resembles a scientific weapon that a paranormal magazine claimed was sent to this planet by aliens.

991.name: Iron-bundle
991.flavorText: Its shape is similar to a robot featured in a paranormal magazine article. The robot was said to have been created by an ancient civilization.

992.name: Iron-hands
992.flavorText: It is very similar to a cyborg covered exclusively by a paranormal magazine. The cyborg was said to be the modified form of a certain athlete.

993.name: Iron-jugulis
993.flavorText: It resembles a certain Pokémon introduced in a paranormal magazine, described as the offspring of a Hydreigon that fell in love with a robot.

994.name: Iron-moth
994.flavorText: This Pokémon resembles an unknown object described in a paranormal magazine as a UFO sent to observe humanity.

995.name: Iron-thorns
995.flavorText: It has some similarities to a Pokémon introduced in a dubious magazine as a Tyranitar from one billion years into the future.

996.name: Frigibax
996.flavorText: Frigibax absorbs heat through its dorsal fin and converts the heat into ice energy. The higher the temperature, the more energy Frigibax stores.

997.name: Arctibax
997.flavorText: Arctibax freezes the air around it, protecting its face with an ice mask and turning its dorsal fin into a blade of ice.

998.name: Baxcalibur
998.flavorText: This Pokémon blasts cryogenic air out from its mouth. This air can instantly freeze even liquid-hot lava.

999.name: Gimmighoul
999.flavorText: This Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago. It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.

1000.name: Gholdengo
1000.flavorText: Its body seems to be made up of 1,000 coins. This Pokémon gets along well with others and is quick to make friends with anybody.

1001.name: Wo-chien
1001.flavorText: The grudge of a person punished for writing the king’s evil deeds upon wooden tablets has clad itself in dead leaves to become a Pokémon.

1002.name: Chien-pao
1002.flavorText: This Pokémon can control 100 tons of fallen snow. It plays around innocently by leaping in and out of avalanches it has caused.

1003.name: Ting-lu
1003.flavorText: The fear poured into an ancient ritual vessel has clad itself in rocks and dirt to become a Pokémon.

1004.name: Chi-yu
1004.flavorText: It controls flames burning at over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit. It casually swims through the sea of lava it creates by melting rock and sand.

1005.name: Roaring-moon
1005.flavorText: It is possible that this is the creature listed as Roaring Moon in an expedition journal that still holds many mysteries.

1006.name: Iron-valiant
1006.flavorText: It has some similarities to a mad scientist’s invention covered in a paranormal magazine.

1007.name: Koraidon
1007.flavorText: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

1008.name: Miraidon
1008.flavorText: Much remains unknown about this creature. It resembles Cyclizar, but it is far more ruthless and powerful.

1009.name: Walking-wake
1009.flavorText: This ferocious creature is shrouded in mystery. It's named after an aquatic monster mentioned in an old expedition journal.

1010.name: Iron-leaves
1010.flavorText: Many of its physical characteristics match those of a Virizion from the future that was covered in a paranormal magazine.

1011.name: Dipplin
1011.flavorText: Dipplin is two creatures in one Pokémon. Its evolution was triggered by a special apple grown only in one place.

1012.name: Poltchageist
1012.flavorText: Supposedly, the regrets of a tea ceremony master who died before perfecting his craft lingered in some matcha and became a Pokémon.

1013.name: Sinistcha
1013.flavorText: It pretends to be tea, trying to fool people into drinking it so it can drain their life-force. Its ruse is generally unsuccessful.

1014.name: Okidogi
1014.flavorText: After all its muscles were stimulated by the toxic chain around its neck, Okidogi transformed and gained a powerful physique.

1015.name: Munkidori
1015.flavorText: The chain is made from toxins that enhance capabilities. It stimulated Munkidori's brain and caused the Pokémon's psychic powers to bloom.

1016.name: Fezandipiti
1016.flavorText: Fezandipiti owes its beautiful looks and lovely voice to the toxic stimulants emanating from the chain wrapped around its body.

1017.name: Ogerpon
1017.flavorText: This Pokémon's type changes based on which mask it's wearing. It confounds its enemies with nimble movements and kicks.

1018.name: Archaludon
1018.flavorText: It gathers static electricity from its surroundings. The beams it launches when down on all fours are tremendously powerful.

1019.name: Hydrapple
1019.flavorText: Seven syrpents live inside an apple made of syrup. The syrpent in the center is the commander.

1020.name: Gouging-fire
1020.flavorText: There are scant few reports of this creature being sighted. One short video shows it rampaging and spouting pillars of flame.

1021.name: Raging-bolt
1021.flavorText: It's said to incinerate everything around it with lightning launched from its fur. Very little is known about this creature.

1022.name: Iron-boulder
1022.flavorText: It resembles a Pokémon described in a dubious magazine as a Terrakion that had been modified by an evil organization.

1023.name: Iron-crown
1023.flavorText: It resembles a mysterious object introduced in a paranormal magazine as a cutting-edge weapon shaped like a Cobalion.

1024.name: Terapagos
1024.flavorText: Terapagos protects itself using its power to transform energy into hard crystals. This Pokémon is the source of the Terastal phenomenon.

1025.name: Pecharunt
1025.flavorText: It feeds others toxic mochi that draw out desires and capabilities. Those who eat the mochi fall under Pecharunt’s control, chained to its will.
